Cost discrepancies were found across multiple reporting sources (API, billing section, campaign analytics), inflating earlier ROI calculations by up to 4.5x.

kb_faqs.question: What was the WhatsApp channel performance for boAt campaigns and what was its cost contribution?
kb_faqs.answer: WhatsApp health dropped to RED within 3 days of campaign start and had to be disabled; it was restored only after 1 week by which most campaign activity was complete. Only 7 leads responded on WhatsApp total, and out of 300 hot/warm leads only 3 engaged via WhatsApp. WhatsApp cost was ₹204 out of ₹12,240 total campaign cost (1.6%), and all 10 store visits/conversions came from voice calling alone.

kb_faqs.question: What was the spend distribution by lead interest category for boAt, and what insight did it reveal?
kb_faqs.answer: Spend distribution showed: Hot leads ₹2.2k, Warm leads ₹2.5k, Cold leads ₹672, and Not-interested/dissatisfied leads ₹3.4k. Disproportionate spend was going to dissatisfied leads unlikely to convert, driven by the bot continuing to pitch rather than exiting. The top 20 longest-duration calls included 7 from 'not interested' customers where the bot attempted to resolve issues without success, directly inflating cost.

kb_faqs.question: What were the connectivity and qualification metrics for the boAt Sense campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: In the Sense campaign, overall connectivity was 71%: AI call only 56.8%, WhatsApp only 5%, and Call+WhatsApp 9.4%. Marketing template delivery rate was 53%, expected to rise to 90–95% with utility templates. Top 50 leads out of 1000 contributed to 26% of overall voicebot consumption, and 41 leads had conversations exceeding 5 minutes.

kb_faqs.question: What were Frido's UAT performance metrics vs. human benchmark?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido's bot achieved 37% conversion vs. human agent's 17–25%, with a CPC of ₹29 vs. human agent CPC of ₹88. After billing corrections, campaign cost changed by only ~₹8, keeping CPC at ₹29. The next campaign was planned on 450 leads with a flow change that needed internal testing before triggering.

kb_faqs.question: What were the results of the Bhanzu pilot campaign and what configuration changes were planned?
kb_faqs.answer: Bhanzu's new script focused on demo booking only, with changes to initial rapport-building content. Out of a sample of 20 qualified leads: 7 were disqualified due to laptop unavailability, 1 for English proficiency, 1 didn't pick up for the demo, and 1 completed the demo. A policy change was confirmed by Bhanzu's CRO allowing phone-based attendance, making the 7 laptop-disqualified leads now eligible, expanding the recoverable lead pool.

kb_faqs.question: What was the second campaign WhatsApp connectivity rate for 91Trucks and what did it imply?
kb_faqs.answer: Only 1.5% of leads connected via WhatsApp in the second 91Trucks campaign even after sending 3–4 messages to everyone, making WhatsApp marketing negligible for this use case. WhatsApp added only ₹0.5 per lead to the ₹8.2 base cost. All conversion data showed no WhatsApp-attributed conversions; the team decided to focus on voice calling.

kb_faqs.question: What issues were discovered with HealthTotal lead data quality and how did it impact the pilot?
kb_faqs.answer: HealthTotal shared a mix of old and fresh leads instead of the agreed 6-months-old leads, and many leads were existing enrolled members who had already paid — human agents were not even calling them. Of 700 leads total, 600 had already been consumed (50 UAT-1 + 50 UAT-2 + 500 Campaign-1), leaving only ~100 valid leads. The client's claimed 60% conversion benchmark was also found to be unverified, and no further campaigns were to run until lead quality and human benchmarks were clarified.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Handpickd campaign observations and what conversion benchmark existed?
kb_faqs.answer: Best-performing human agent conversion for Handpickd was 2.5% (single-day data, 5th Feb). The campaign involved 3 historical campaigns (Onion & Tomato, Free Trial Pass, Wallet Recharge), and conversions improved on weekends. The wallet recharge offer changed from ₹100 cap to 50% cashback on any first recharge, and the free combo basket offer increased from ₹99 to ₹200 — requiring prompt redesign before the next 450-lead campaign.

kb_faqs.question: What was the S4S use case assessment and why was it deemed unsuitable for Convin Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: S4S called Google Listing restaurant numbers (mostly landlines) to get owner/chef mobile numbers from receptionists — the target PoC was not directly dialable. Out of 100 attempts, ~50 connected and 25 demos were scheduled, but the bot cannot capture the 10-digit owner number mid-call or automatically dial them. Convin Sense cannot replicate this human-driven middle step, so the project was put on hold for 2 weeks pending S4S starting paid ads campaigns where direct leads would be available.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Ezo/Frido campaign connectivity observations?
kb_faqs.answer: In one Ezo campaign, approximately 1,700 out of 2,400 leads did not respond in any way (low connectivity). Out of 467 leads that connected on call at least once, 264 were classified as hot/warm, suggesting the bot performed well once connected. The primary issue was connectivity rather than bot performance quality.

kb_faqs.question: What was the critical billing/cost calculation bug discovered and what was its impact?
kb_faqs.answer: Campaign cost data from three sources — campaign analytics dashboard, billing section, and Lovelesh's API — all showed different values. The API originally used 'ai_call_minutes' for cost calculation, but a later update added 'pulses' as a separate parameter without updating the cost formula, causing cost to be understated by up to 4.5x in some calculations. This bug impacted ROI analyses for boAt and 91Trucks; the spend tab in analytics was designated as the single source of truth, with the cost-reporting APIs to be deprecated.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Lead Interest Prompt auto-reset bug and its potential impact?
kb_faqs.answer: The Lead Interest Prompt was automatically reverting to the default prompt across campaigns (observed in both Handpicked and HealthTotal campaigns). This meant active campaigns could silently run with incorrect lead qualification logic. Teams were alerted to manually verify their Lead Interest Prompt in all active campaigns as an immediate workaround (Jira: AC-1197).

kb_faqs.question: What was the Avataar bot behavior issues found during internal testing?
kb_faqs.answer: Four issues were identified: (1) bot didn't end call even after customer said 'not interested' multiple times — fixed by adding a hard directive under Pillar 2 to disengage after multiple disinterest signals; (2) brief silence followed by 'Hello' triggered conversation restart — fixed with explicit prompt instruction preventing restarts; (3) voice (Shristi v3) described as too bold/aggressive for sales qualification; (4) 'मैं' pronounced as 'मैन' — a consistent TTS pronunciation issue across Cartesia voices.

kb_faqs.question: What TTS pronunciation bug was reported for English words and what was the impact?
kb_faqs.answer: All TTS voices pronounced the English word 'do' as Hindi 'दो'. Replacing 'do' with 'डू' caused issues with derived words like 'does' and 'don't'. Even replacing 'does' with 'डज़' still produced mispronunciation as 'डू-एस'. The product team's guidance was to set preferred language to English for English-language bots as the configuration fix.

kb_faqs.question: What bug caused silent calls from the bot's perspective vs. customer's perspective?
kb_faqs.answer: In one documented case (Jira: AC-1224), the customer answered but the bot was silent for ~30 seconds at full volume — yet the call recording showed the bot's audio was present. This means operational teams could incorrectly conclude the customer disconnected due to no bot engagement when the issue was on the customer's audio delivery side. Product noted this as a rare but known issue, escalated to Exotel.

kb_faqs.question: What bug existed with leads being incorrectly marked as 'Completed' despite goal not being achieved?
kb_faqs.answer: In both HealthTotal (Jira: AC-1196) and 91Trucks (Jira: AC-1243), leads were being marked as 'Completed' even when the campaign goal was not actually achieved. This caused inaccurate campaign reporting and inflated success metrics. Both tickets were escalated to Lovelesh for investigation.

kb_faqs.question: What call duration reporting bugs were found in the Lead Outcome Report?
kb_faqs.answer: Two separate duration bugs were reported: (1) A call showing 32 seconds in the Lead Outcome Report was actually 19 seconds when reviewed in recording (Jira: AC-1210, HealthTotal); (2) A similar call duration mismatch was filed for another client (Jira: AC-1213). Additionally, the Lead Outcome Report showed '0 Replies on WA' for leads that had actual WhatsApp replies visible in the platform (Jira: AC-1212).

kb_faqs.question: What was the WhatsApp WABA ban issue with PW and what caused it?
kb_faqs.answer: PW's WABA was blocked by Meta with no action possible for 72 hours. Multiple templates were in use — some utility, some marketing — and if a utility template got banned, the fallback was a marketing template. The WABA health was still showing as 'high' before the ban, providing no early warning signal. The team identified checking the number of users who blocked the account as the only available RCA signal.

kb_faqs.question: What was the agent cloning bug and what was the workaround?
kb_faqs.answer: When cloning an existing agent, the AI call integration was not being attached to the cloned agent (Jira: AC-1225). The workaround was to create a new agent from scratch with the same settings as the original rather than using the clone function. This was flagged to the tech team for a fix.

kb_faqs.question: What issue was found with 'not connected' leads being misclassified in lead interest categories?
kb_faqs.answer: Not-connected leads were being incorrectly classified as 'Not Interested' or 'Cold' instead of remaining uncategorized or in a separate bucket. This had reportedly been fixed in a prior release but continued to persist. The issue was re-raised on Jira (AC-1123) and escalated to Lovelesh.

kb_faqs.question: What Vobiz telephony integration bug was discovered during testing?
kb_faqs.answer: During test calls on the Ezo Books account using Vobiz telephony, the bot kept repeating the end-call statement endlessly in a loop. The issue was flagged to Raajit and the tech team for fixing, with an update to be shared once resolved.

kb_faqs.question: What STT (speech-to-text) errors were identified during Avataar bot analysis?
kb_faqs.answer: STT issues included 'slimming' being transcribed as 'swimming' and 'cancel' as 'cancer', which affected context passed to the LLM and caused downstream hallucinations in bot responses. Additionally, the bot was not correctly conveying amounts and used an aggressive, fast-paced tone deemed unsuitable for sales qualification. These findings were from a manual audit shared by the Data Labs team.

kb_faqs.question: What was the language switching bug on Miles and how was it handled?
kb_faqs.answer: Miles faced a language switching issue where the bot was not correctly switching languages during calls. A fix was released on 27th January (Jira: AC-1056), but the issue continued to persist in the team's testing as of 5th Feb. The product team confirmed it was a configuration issue — setting 'preferred language' to English resolved it for English-only bots, and Sudesha confirmed it worked after testing.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Allen C2C (Click-to-Call) issue and what was the suspected root cause?
kb_faqs.answer: C2C was enabled for Allen but agents and customers could not hear each other (Jira: AC-1194). The suspected root cause was IP whitelisting, similar to an issue previously encountered and resolved at ABSLI. The ticket was assigned to Lovelesh and Raajit for investigation.

kb_faqs.question: What was the NBA (Next Best Action) generation issue reported for HealthTotal?
kb_faqs.answer: No NBA was generated for 2 consecutive days for the HealthTotal account (Jira: AC-1208). This was filed by Ashutosh and assigned to Lovelesh for investigation. No resolution details were shared in the messages.

kb_faqs.question: What was the WABA 24-hour rolling window limit issue?
kb_faqs.answer: Meta enforces limits at the WABA level on ongoing conversations (defined as leads in a 24-hour opt-in rolling window). One campaign hit this limit, blocking further WhatsApp sends. The setting is visible in the WABA settings page under '24 hour rolling window limit'. This is a Meta-level constraint that requires WABA-level management.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Exotel virtual/backup number issue affecting caller ID and how was it handled?
kb_faqs.answer: When new Exotel numbers were procured, calls were being displayed to customers from a backup number rather than the procured number for the first 2–8 days. One backup number was already marked as spam for Frido. The team switched to Vobiz telephony as an alternative, mailed Exotel to remove backup numbers, and asked the client SPOC not to whitelist the Exotel numbers until new Vobiz numbers were provided.

kb_faqs.question: What was the DND false-positive issue with Exotel numbers?
kb_faqs.answer: Amith's test number was being flagged as DND on the Frido domain even though it wasn't actually on the DND list, blocking internal testing. The resolution was to email hello@exotel.in with the account SID (convin3m) requesting removal of the number from the DND list.

kb_faqs.question: What bulk lead upload queue issue was encountered for ABSLI and Leap?
kb_faqs.answer: Bulk lead upload files were stuck in queue for ABSLI-LI for over 30 minutes (Jira: AC-1219), with the client waiting to load leads. A separate queue issue also blocked campaign summary report downloads for Leap. Both were assigned to Lovelesh for resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What cost calculation issue existed with failed WhatsApp messages?
kb_faqs.answer: Failed WhatsApp messages were being included in campaign cost calculations, inflating the actual spend. Additionally, LLM credits were being consumed for 100% of marketing message attempts even though delivery rate was only ~50% due to Meta restrictions. The decision was made not to charge clients for undelivered messages, and an optimization was raised to use programmatically-filled templates instead of LLM-generated ones to reduce costs.

kb_faqs.question: How should campaign cost be calculated and what is the single source of truth?
kb_faqs.answer: The campaign 'Spend' tab in the analytics dashboard is the designated single source of truth for campaign costs. The Lovelesh-shared API and billing section campaign cost are no longer reliable and the APIs are being deprecated. LMF cost is not included in the spend tab total and must be added manually when calculating ROI. Failed WhatsApp messages should be excluded from cost calculations charged to clients.

kb_faqs.question: How is number reshuffling configured within a single campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: To configure multiple caller IDs for a campaign: procure numbers in the telephony provider, go to the Caller ID section and enter all procured numbers, then save. In the campaign settings under Channel Config, a Caller ID dropdown will show all available numbers — select all to allocate them to the campaign for automatic reshuffling.

kb_faqs.question: How can different WABA numbers be assigned to different campaigns within the same organization?
kb_faqs.answer: Different numbers within the same WABA integration can be assigned to different campaigns. Once an additional number is added to the WABA integration settings, it reflects in the campaign's channel configuration dropdown. This is useful for clients like Ezo where different campaigns need separate WhatsApp numbers.

kb_faqs.question: What configuration decision was made for Miles regarding voice selection?
kb_faqs.answer: Miles had previously used Anika voice on ElevenLabs and liked it. When the platform moved to Cartesia, Vidhya created an instant clone of that voice on Cartesia named 'Avira'. The client was asked to test both 'Avira' and 'Anika' on Cartesia to select their preferred voice. The client had complained about voices being too robotic, driving the exploration of new voice options.

kb_faqs.question: What voice management cleanup was done on the platform?
kb_faqs.answer: Multiple voices were deleted to clean up the platform: Durga2a, Durga1a, Durga version 2, Riya4a, Riya2a, Riya1a, Shrashti_TTS were scheduled for deletion. 'Pranika v2' was retained. Two new voices were added: 'Debasmita' and 'Shimona'. Teams were given a 2-hour window to flag any in-use voices before deletion.

kb_faqs.question: What was the boAt campaign optimization decision regarding dissatisfied customers?
kb_faqs.answer: The boAt team agreed to cut short the 'not interested' pitch — once a customer expresses disinterest, the bot should thank them and disconnect rather than continuing to engage. The boAt team wanted dissatisfied leads bifurcated into two buckets: those who said nothing on call vs. those who expressed product dissatisfaction. This change was estimated to bring CPC down from ₹158 to ~₹116 by reducing the ₹3.4k spend on not-interested leads to ~₹1.3k.

kb_faqs.question: What was the decision on utility vs. marketing WhatsApp templates for boAt?
kb_faqs.answer: The boAt team wanted to try utility templates since they have customer consent. Utility templates would increase delivery rate from 53% to an expected 90–95%, with a call limit of 4 and WhatsApp limit of 2 per lead (vs. 3 for marketing). The team agreed to run one campaign with utility templates on a trial basis with a 2-day campaign duration, with the caveat that this approach may not scale due to WABA account health risks.

kb_faqs.question: What bot goal configuration issue was identified for boAt's review collection use case?
kb_faqs.answer: The current goal required customers to confirm they had posted a review during the call, leading to extended conversations and high AHT. A proposed change was to set the goal as just getting customer agreement to post a review, with human agents following up on actual posting — this was identified as the easiest cost optimization to implement. Data showed the average review can be completed in under 10 WhatsApp messages and ~3 minutes of talk time.

kb_faqs.question: What is the proposed total call duration cap feature for cost optimization?
kb_faqs.answer: A feature request was raised to cap overall voicebot cost per lead by setting a limit on total call duration across all attempts (not just per-call duration). The suggested cap was 7–8 minutes total per lead, which would prevent outlier leads from consuming disproportionate cost. This feature doesn't currently exist — the existing max call duration setting only applies per individual call, not cumulatively.

kb_faqs.question: What Avataar bot configuration issues were identified and how were they fixed using the 8-Pillar framework?
kb_faqs.answer: Using the AACC tool, Amit identified that the LLM wasn't reliably executing disinterest-handling instructions from Pillar 3 (KB). Fix: a hard non-negotiable directive was added to Pillar 2 (call mindset) to immediately disengage after multiple 'not interested' signals. For conversation restart on silence, an explicit prompt instruction was added to Pillar 2 preventing restarts and enforcing direct closure. The 8-Pillar framework was confirmed as being released to production.

kb_faqs.question: What dialling strategy SOP gap was identified and what was the planned resolution?
kb_faqs.answer: There was no documented SOP for the 'dialling strategy from human agents post-qualification' — agents were dialling from personal phones and sending WhatsApp messages informally, making tracking difficult. The principle confirmed was at least 3 daily attempts plus WhatsApp. Vaibhav committed to properly documenting and sharing the dialling strategy with DMs, champions, and floor execution teams.

kb_faqs.question: How did the team handle Kotak's resistance to starting UAT?
kb_faqs.answer: Kotak wanted to continue testing the bot extensively (15-minute test calls with many questions per call) rather than proceeding to UAT. Sudesha raised this with Hassan, who agreed to call the Kotak SPOC (Shree) directly to push for UAT approval. No resolution was documented in the messages beyond this escalation.

kb_faqs.question: How did the team handle Handpickd's concern about incorrect Warm lead classification?
kb_faqs.answer: Handpickd flagged that leads with only 15–20 seconds of interaction were being classified as Warm, which they felt was inaccurate. The proposed solutions were: (1) improve classification logic accuracy; (2) switch to entity-based classification instead of lead scoring which requires more tuning; (3) upgrade the domain to a better LLM model for accuracy. Bharat suggested avoiding lead scoring for pilots entirely and relying on entities instead. Vidhya agreed to connect with Ashutosh to review the Lead Interest Prompt and close on the approach.

kb_faqs.question: How did the team handle Handpickd's reluctance to follow through on Click2Call and sharing benchmarks?
kb_faqs.answer: Despite multiple requests over a week, Handpickd had not implemented Click2Call from the platform and had not shared conversion benchmark numbers. Ashutosh created a CSV sheet himself for lead tracking since the client's own sheet wasn't shared. Hassan escalated and committed to calling Himanshu directly the next day for Click2Call implementation and benchmark numbers. The client's management mandated contacting only genuinely interested leads — Ashutosh had to convince them to follow up even on warm leads.

kb_faqs.question: How was HealthTotal's conversion benchmark confusion handled?
kb_faqs.answer: HealthTotal initially claimed 60% conversion but later couldn't confirm it was the right number. On a direct call with Rohit and Saurabh, Rohit acknowledged this was a cold funnel without sufficient data to comment on performance at this stage. Rohit confirmed he would compile and share human benchmarks by the next day. No further campaign was to run until lead quality was validated (existing enrolled members removed) and benchmarks were provided.

kb_faqs.question: How did the team respond to Shaadi's request for international calling capability?
kb_faqs.answer: Shaadi requested that the pilot scope be expanded to include calling international prospects in addition to domestic calls. The team pushed back citing current pilot scope limitations and different calling setup requirements for international numbers. Bhargava committed to keeping the team updated on Shaadi's response, and Kushaal confirmed not to do anything beyond current pilot scope.

kb_faqs.question: How was the Frido conversion rate alignment concern handled?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido's bot was converting at 37% vs. their human baseline of ~17%, which Kushaal flagged as potentially misaligned — the client might question why bot conversion was significantly higher. The approach was to speak with the client to explain how natural conversion and bot-nudged conversion were being attributed. Kushaal noted that benchmark alignment should happen before UAT in future engagements to prevent disconnect at results-sharing stage.

kb_faqs.question: What feature request was raised for image/attachment support in the human WhatsApp flow?
kb_faqs.answer: A feature request (Jira: AC-1200) was raised to allow human agents to send attachments or images in the human WhatsApp flow — for example, confirmation screenshots. This was identified as necessary to drive adoption of messaging from the Convin platform rather than agents using external channels. Tagged to Lovelesh and Raajit for consideration.

kb_faqs.question: What feature was requested for lead-level system message customization during bulk upload?
kb_faqs.answer: A feature request (Jira: AC-1215) was raised to allow different system messages to be configured for different leads during bulk upload, rather than a single system message applied to all leads in a campaign. This was raised by Shivansh from the Conversation Design team.

kb_faqs.question: What dashboard filtering feature was requested?
kb_faqs.answer: A feature request (Jira: AC-1216) was raised for entity-based filtering directly from the dashboard, allowing teams to filter leads/results by entity values without needing API access. Separately, a custom date range filter for the campaign spend page was requested — currently it shows data but doesn't allow saving custom date range filters.

kb_faqs.question: What billing transparency features were requested for the campaign spend page?
kb_faqs.answer: Beyond total cost, teams needed number of pulses and number of WhatsApp messages broken out separately on the spend page to allow recalculation under different pricing tiers. LMF cost was also not included in the campaign spend total and needed to be added. Lovelesh committed to making pulse and message counts visible in the spend page by evening.

kb_faqs.question: What coupon delivery automation was identified as a key feature need for boAt?
kb_faqs.answer: Post-review coupon communication was generating a high volume of repeat customer queries because the bot verbally confirmed a coupon would be sent but no automated delivery was triggered. The recommended solution was direct system integration to automatically trigger and deliver coupon codes upon review completion, which would significantly reduce repeat queries and conversational overhead.

kb_faqs.question: What overall call duration cap feature was requested for cost optimization?
kb_faqs.answer: Teams requested a configurable total call duration cap per lead (across all attempts, not just per call) to prevent outlier leads from consuming disproportionate voicebot cost. The suggested limit was 7–8 minutes total per lead. This feature doesn't currently exist — the existing setting only caps duration per individual call.

kb_faqs.question: What new onboarding process was defined to improve customer experience post-sales?
kb_faqs.answer: The updated process requires: Sales to initiate internal kickoff immediately when a deal is likely (not waiting for MSA signing); 5 working days of knowledge transfer (KT) for any CSM before leading a project; PM to send a KT completion email to CSM marking Vaibhav, Biswatma, and Mrugank in CC along with the pilot deck and KT document; CSM to confirm KT completion via email; then client kickoff happens. Sales team was asked to flag any foreseen closures the following week for KT planning.

kb_faqs.question: What ROI communication challenge existed with boAt's cost-per-review calculation?
kb_faqs.answer: There was ambiguity on whether the human agent CPC was ₹49 or ₹110, as Sonia said she would get back on this but hadn't. The ₹49 figure was calculated as reviews posted divided by (total leads minus not-connected, negative, and neutral leads) with agent salary at ₹32k, but the full cost including external factors was ~₹50k, yielding a CPC closer to ₹90. This needed to be clarified before presenting the ROI comparison to the boAt team.

kb_faqs.question: What was the pricing decision on undelivered WhatsApp marketing messages?
kb_faqs.answer: Convin decided not to charge clients for WhatsApp marketing messages that failed to deliver due to Meta restrictions. Convin incurred the cost but would absorb it rather than passing it to the client. This was confirmed by Ashish (leadership). An LLM cost optimization was also planned — using programmatically-filled templates instead of LLM-generated ones for marketing messages to reduce costs on undelivered messages.

kb_faqs.question: What were the key bot quality issues observed during Avataar's pilot that led to founder dissatisfaction?
kb_faqs.answer: Avataar's founder Saumya flagged multiple issues: bot skipped the brand introduction and jumped straight to profiling, mispronounced 'Are' as 'Arey' and 'Do' as 'डू', had 4-second latency due to WSS connection drops with Cartesia TTS, and suggested a past time slot for appointment booking. Deepanshu (another tester) experienced the bot calling him 'Maam' throughout, bot going silent for ~2 seconds ignoring his question, voice breaking randomly, and the bot hanging up mid-call twice. Both calls had issues, and Saumya was not confident enough to proceed to contract without quality improvement.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Boat (boAt) campaign performance metrics and cost-per-conversion (CPC) improvements across campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Campaign C1 had a spend of ₹7,079 with 35 bot-only conversions, giving a CPC of ₹202 (bot only) or ₹158 (bot + human). Campaign C3 after optimization had a spend of ₹3,616.75 with 32 bot-only conversions, giving a CPC of ₹113 — approximately a 50% reduction in CPC with nearly the same conversion count. Final combined numbers (bot + human) for C3 showed 37 bot conversions + 19 human conversions = 56 total, with a CPC of ₹85.11, and 133 leads still pending human calling.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Ezo pilot's latency profile and how was dead air quantified?
kb_faqs.answer: Ezo had 4,930 connected calls, of which 80% had average latency under 3 seconds. To identify dead air issues, the team tracked that 1,230 calls had users saying 'hello' at least once, and 695 calls had users saying 'hello' more than once — the latter being a proxy indicator for bot silence exceeding acceptable limits. The Ezo founder raised the issue of dead air exceeding 5 seconds, and the team proposed a Dead Air Report with configurable duration settings as a way to prove performance data to the client.

kb_faqs.question: What caused the 4-second latency issue in Avataar calls and what was the root cause identified?
kb_faqs.answer: The latency was caused by the WSS (WebSocket Secure) connection with Cartesia TTS getting disconnected and reconnected multiple times during calls. This was logged under Jira ticket AC-1258 and was prioritized for fix the following morning. The same latency pattern had been observed and ticketed previously for Boat and Miles Education pilots as well.

kb_faqs.question: What was the end statement looping bug, which clients were affected, and what was the quick fix?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot was looping its end/closing statement repeatedly instead of hanging up after the final message, and this was confirmed to be independent of the dialer (occurred on both Exotel and other dialers). The issue was happening in 3 out of 3 tested calls for at least one client (Ezo/Dhanda). The quick fix identified by Raajit was to remove the human handoff tool ('Dhanda_AI_Customer_Handoff' or 'Call_Transfer_Dhanda_Sales') from the agent configuration. A longer-term fix was tracked under Jira.

kb_faqs.question: What was the pronunciation issue with words like 'do', 'are', and 'may', and how was it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: Words like 'Do', 'Are', and 'May' were being mispronounced by the TTS engine — 'Do' was pronounced as 'डू' in some contexts impacting words like 'doctor' and 'doesn't', 'Are' as 'Arey', and 'May' as 'Mai'. Raajit implemented default word replacements across all tenants and confirmed case-sensitive whole-word matching (e.g., 'US' vs 'us') to avoid conflicts. Additional words can be configured in Settings > Integrations > TTS, and it was noted that changing 'do' to 'डू' could cascade to affect 'does', so 'does' also needed a separate replacement (e.g., 'डज़').

kb_faqs.question: What was the issue with sending two WhatsApp messages back-to-back within 10 seconds?
kb_faqs.answer: When a customer sent two messages within 10 seconds, the system wasn't handling them correctly — it was generating a response to the first message without consolidating the second. The proposed product solution was: if a new customer message arrives before the bot has sent its response to the earlier one, cancel the pending response and regenerate a new NBA (Next Best Action) using both messages as combined input, with a cap of 5 back-to-back messages to avoid infinite loops. A Jira ticket was raised and Lovelesh was assigned to implement this.

kb_faqs.question: What was the 'hello mid-conversation' bug observed in Leap and what happened?
kb_faqs.answer: In the Leap pilot, when a user said 'hello' in the middle of a conversation, the bot would restart from the beginning of the conversation flow instead of continuing from where it left off. This was logged under Jira ticket AC-1270 and escalated to Raajit for resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Bhanzu WhatsApp campaign failure and what caused it?
kb_faqs.answer: In Bhanzu's latest campaign, all 280 message attempts failed due to a payment error in their WhatsApp Business Account (WABA). Similarly, Guvi's campaign of 8,221 attempts had ~5,000 failures — 10% due to payment issues and 85% due to marketing template rejection, resulting in only ~35% delivery rate. The team advised changing the template and resolving the WABA payment to resume campaigns.

kb_faqs.question: What was the analytics page filter bug flagged during the Ezo pilot?
kb_faqs.answer: The analytics page was throwing an error when the timeline was set to 'Today' and the lead creation date was filtered to the current date (13th Feb), as flagged by the Ezo client and logged under Jira AC-1305. Even after the error was fixed, the campaign summary was still showing aggregate data for all 2,564 leads instead of just the ~135 leads added that day, indicating the date filter was not being applied to the summary report. A workaround was shown to the client using the campaign page for filtered lead outcome data.

kb_faqs.question: What was the WABA phone number ID bug affecting the Boat campaign and how was it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: The Boat WABA campaign was failing because the phone number ID field had been set to empty, preventing the system from fetching details required to send messages. Lovelesh identified and fixed this, confirming the campaign could proceed. The root cause of why the phone number ID became empty was still under investigation.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Hindi pronunciation issue where 'Main' was being pronounced as 'Mei' in bot responses?
kb_faqs.answer: The issue was that the LLM was generating Hindi language responses in Roman script (e.g., 'main') instead of Devanagari script ('मैं'), causing TTS to mispronounce it. Raajit clarified this was not a TTS word replacement issue but a prompt-level issue — the LLM should be instructed to generate Hindi in Devanagari script. However, changing this in the 8-pillar prompt would break WhatsApp text messaging, so it needed to be handled at the voicebot level separately. A ticket (AC-1316) was raised.

kb_faqs.question: What was the variable mapping product gap and how was it being worked around?
kb_faqs.answer: The platform lacked proper variable mapping between CRM integration fields and Convin Sense's internal fields — custom fields received via API were not automatically mappable to bot script variables, requiring a manual roundabout solution. For Ezo, the workaround was to pass the custom field (e.g., mx_Business_Name) in the API payload and reference it in the prompt text, letting the LLM interpret it, though Raajit acknowledged this has lower reliability than code-level mapping. Bhargava confirmed the same approach was used for Shaadi.com, where data accuracy was impacted. A proper fix was planned as a product priority.

kb_faqs.question: What prompt size limits were enforced and why?
kb_faqs.answer: Raajit explicitly flagged that prompts cannot be 50 pages long and that 88,000-character prompts should not be expected to yield good outcomes. For Avataar, Ashutosh reduced the prompt from ~95,000 characters to ~25,000–30,000 characters as part of the redesign. The recommended approach for large knowledge bases is to use the RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) tool instead of embedding all information directly in the prompt.

kb_faqs.question: What changes were made to the Avataar bot prompt redesign and what accuracy improvements were observed?
kb_faqs.answer: The Avataar prompt was redesigned for Laser Hair Reduction (LHR) specifically, reducing size from ~95k to ~25k-30k characters. Key changes included: adding a 'Fresh Interaction' instruction improving intro accuracy to ~85%, making the bot gender-neutral (no sir/ma'am) to eliminate gendering errors, removing incorrect 'plant-based products' mention from KB, adding explicit appointment scheduling logic including handling 'abhi book karva do' type requests, and fixing the introduction adherence. The bot was also switched from the Srishti voice to Artika voice which was identified as cleaner.

kb_faqs.question: What was the recommended approach for controlling bot opening lines, and what workaround was used?
kb_faqs.answer: Clients generally expected a consistent, near-verbatim opening line on the first call, with dynamic context-aware openings for subsequent calls. The workaround used by Amith was adding an explicit instruction in the S0 state prompt: 'The opening line in this state is a fixed template and must be used verbatim. No rewording, paraphrasing, reordering, or additional descriptors are allowed' followed by the exact verbatim line. Vidhya from Product noted that the static opening line type setting could be used, and confirmed that first-call static + subsequent-call dynamic behavior is what clients expect. Sudesha also noted that a short summary of prior interaction would be ideal for follow-up calls.

kb_faqs.question: What was the configuration decision to handle the Boat bot's improved CPC performance?
kb_faqs.answer: The key configuration change for Boat's C3 campaign was making the bot less empathetic and more focused — it was configured to engage only happy customers for review collection and redirect unhappy customers to Boat's support channel instead of trying to handle complaints. This 'ghost on complaints, engage on happy' approach resulted in the ~50% CPC reduction from ₹202 to ₹113 in bot-only conversions.

kb_faqs.question: What was the decision on Truecaller whitelisting and how should it be handled going forward?
kb_faqs.answer: Convin had done Truecaller whitelisting for 91Trucks as a test but it costs ₹15,000 per month and was not intended as a standard offering. Vaibhav confirmed this should not be offered for free to clients — if clients need it, they can get it done themselves through their own Truecaller premium subscription, or the sales team can price it separately. PMs were instructed not to commit to this without sales team involvement.

kb_faqs.question: What was the issue and fix decision around Ezo's telephony integration with two dialers?
kb_faqs.answer: Ezo was using two dialers — Acefone and SlashRTC — and requested custom telephony integration with both. The internal decision was to not do two integrations for free during pilot; one integration is standard, and any additional integrations should be charged on a man-days basis. Ashish and Raajit were to be involved in the final call, and the team communicated to the client they would revert with a decision by EOD.

kb_faqs.question: What was the approach for appointment scheduling prompts to handle relative time references like 'abhi kara do'?
kb_faqs.answer: The appointment scheduling prompt was designed to ask for date and time as two separate mandatory questions. For relative time references (e.g., 'tomorrow', day names), the bot was instructed to auto-calculate the actual calendar date and confirm it back before asking for time preference. For immediate requests like 'abhi kara do', Ashutosh added explicit handling in the prompt to address such cases, improving accuracy. Raajit also proposed adding calendar check and web search tools for current time to avoid the bot suggesting past time slots.

kb_faqs.question: How did Shaadi.com respond to the bot pilot and what was the key concern raised?
kb_faqs.answer: Shaadi.com's SPOC Surabhi called in saying the bot felt 'more like an IVR' and that the standalone voice bot demo shown earlier by Ashutosh was much better than the current Convin Sense experience. She highlighted this technology is part of their AOP (Annual Operating Plan) for the year, making it high priority. She also noted that Vaibhav had not joined after the first call, indicating engagement/responsiveness was being tracked by the client. Bhargava was working on configuration changes and planned to share different samples the same day.

kb_faqs.question: How did ABSLI respond to the MeitY AI regulation circular and how was it handled?
kb_faqs.answer: ABSLI raised concerns about a MeitY amendment to IT rules (effective 20 Feb 2026) around AI-generated/synthetic content, asking if the bot needs to announce itself as a bot. Raajit reviewed the circular and determined it specifically addresses social media content and doesn't strictly apply to Convin's voice bot product. The recommended response was to have the legal team review the circular first, then share the conclusion with the client, supported by a Perplexity AI analysis for quick reference.

kb_faqs.question: What objections did the Bigul pilot client have and what pre-kickoff alignment was recommended?
kb_faqs.answer: Before the Bigul kickoff, Sayani (CSM) flagged several open alignment points: Convin Sense CPC was currently higher than human agents and a target CPC benchmark needed to be set; final DM numbers were pending so success criteria were unclear; bot connectivity rate was much lower than human agents and DND/consent-based calling needed confirmation; and even a single bad call (e.g., TTS issue) was being treated as a blocker with expectations of human-like behavior. Kushaal recommended an in-person meeting with all relevant stakeholders to align on these before kickoff.

kb_faqs.question: What was the feature request around dead air reporting for Ezo?
kb_faqs.answer: Ezo's founder raised concerns about dead air exceeding 5 seconds in calls and asked for proof of performance. The team requested a 'Dead Air Report' similar to what exists in Convin's post-call tool, with a configurable dead air duration setting, to be available in Sense. Vaibhav suggested the metric needed should be '% of calls where there was one silence of 5+ seconds specifically when the bot was supposed to speak' — not just any silence.

kb_faqs.question: What CRM webhook integration was requested and which tenants had already configured it?
kb_faqs.answer: Raajit flagged that only three tenants had configured CRM webhooks from Convin Sense: Ezo, mPokket, and Vyapar. He requested feedback on why other tenants hadn't configured it — whether due to product gaps or client challenges. A follow-up session on integration (similar to the RAG tool KT) was planned for the following week. PW had specific feedback that Utsav was to share.

kb_faqs.question: What was the feature request around bot call opening control?
kb_faqs.answer: The team identified a need for a product-level feature to control the first call's opening line as a static/fixed script, while allowing dynamic context-aware openings for subsequent retry calls. Sudesha additionally requested that follow-up calls include a brief summary of the previous interaction with the lead. Vidhya from Product confirmed this feedback would be implemented accordingly.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Leap pilot's feature request around handling past demo time slots?
kb_faqs.answer: For the Leap pilot, date and time of booked demos were passed to the bot. When the passed time was in the past (i.e., the demo had already occurred), the bot's role should switch from 'will you join the demo?' to 'were you able to attend the demo?' This was working inconsistently — correctly in some cases but failing in others — and Shivansh raised it as a feature/fix request to Raajit.

kb_faqs.question: What was the wallet/billing visibility request from the sales and CSM teams?
kb_faqs.answer: Shivam raised a request that both sales and CSM teams should have visibility into wallet balance, last recharge details, and active plan as per the MSA directly from the dashboard, to enable proactive account management. Ashish confirmed this information is already available in the billing section of the dashboard but acknowledged some data accuracy issues that would be corrected soon.

kb_faqs.question: What onboarding process improvement was requested around deal closures and team readiness?
kb_faqs.answer: Biswa from the onboarding team requested that the sales team provide at least one week's advance notice before planned deal closures so that CSMs can be assigned, KT (knowledge transfer) can be planned, and kickoffs can be better prepared. This came after last-minute closures for ABSLI, Bigul, and Health Total where the onboarding team was caught underprepared. Vaibhav also stressed the need for in-person client meetings for dashboard training, saying virtual training sessions are significantly less effective.

kb_faqs.question: What was the learning around testing bot changes in shared campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: During the Avataar pilot, Amit from Product Delivery was testing with a different prompt on the same campaign as the live bot, causing the introduction to break for real test calls on the final decision day with the client (Gauri). Ashish issued a directive that separate campaigns with separate KBs must always be created for testing — changes should never be made to live campaigns used for actual client calls.

kb_faqs.question: What was the key learning from the Frido campaign launch coordination failure?
kb_faqs.answer: When the Frido campaign was about to launch, it was discovered that Frido's human agents had already started dialing the same leads independently, creating a conflict. The lesson identified was that the team should have recalled/redialed leads in the morning before the campaign start, and coordination with the client's human agent team on lead ownership must be confirmed before any campaign launch. It was also decided that Monday launches are preferable over weekends even if it costs a day, as consistent monitoring improves conversion.

kb_faqs.question: What was the learning around RAG tool usage and large prompts?
kb_faqs.answer: Raajit explicitly warned that 50-page or 88,000-character prompts cannot be expected to produce good outcomes and that RAG tool must be used for large knowledge bases instead of embedding all information in the prompt. A dedicated 30-minute KT session on the RAG tool was scheduled for the Aakash Digital pilot team (Arnab, Lalit, and CSMs) as it was their first time using it. This was planned as a recurring learning session pattern for new features.

kb_faqs.question: What was the LSQ integration learning from the Guvi pilot applied to the Kotak pilot decision?
kb_faqs.answer: During the Guvi pilot, attempting LSQ (LeadSquared) integration caused delays of over one month and was ultimately abandoned in favor of a manual method. When Kotak initiated LSQ integration for lead fetch during their pilot, the team flagged this prior Guvi experience and debated whether to pursue it during pilot phase, given the timeline risk. The team was leaning toward deferring the integration decision and setting expectations with the client.

kb_faqs.question: What was the bot performance comparison between the Avataar standalone demo bot and Convin Sense that became a client concern?
kb_faqs.answer: Shaadi.com's client explicitly said the standalone voice bot that Ashutosh had built earlier was much better than the current Convin Sense experience, which felt 'more like an IVR.' This became a critical issue as Shaadi.com had included this technology in their AOP. The team acknowledged the gap and Bhargava was working on configuration changes to bridge the quality difference.

kb_faqs.question: What did the Ezo call data reveal about bot silence/dead air as a performance indicator?
kb_faqs.answer: Out of 4,930 connected Ezo calls, 1,230 had users saying 'hello' at least once, and 695 had users saying 'hello' more than once — with repeat 'hello' being treated as a proxy indicator of excessive bot silence. This was raised by the Ezo founder who observed dead air of more than 5 seconds in calls. The team was 80% within 3-second average latency but acknowledged the issue and proposed quantifying it through a Dead Air Report.

kb_faqs.question: What voice quality issues were identified with the Srishti TTS voice and what was done?
kb_faqs.answer: The Srishti voice had artifacts and screeching issues during calls. Cartesia had further trained a new version called 'Srishti v6' which was released for testing — the team was asked to test it thoroughly and report back. For Avataar specifically, Raajit recommended switching to the 'Artika' voice as it sounded cleaner than Srishti. The broader goal was to validate Srishti v6 and if successful, apply the same training methodology to all voices.

kb_faqs.question: What ROI framing was proposed to define 'hot leads' attributed to Convin in the sales discussion?
kb_faqs.answer: To establish a clear indicator that a lead purchased because of Convin (rather than other factors), Amith proposed defining 'hot leads' as all people who agreed to use a coupon code to make the purchase — regardless of whether they ultimately used the code. This was suggested as a way to create a defensible attribution metric in sales conversations, though the team acknowledged they shouldn't limit themselves solely to this definition.

kb_faqs.question: What was the team's strategy for presenting Boat's pilot results to the client for deal closure?
kb_faqs.answer: Sudesha suggested running one more campaign before presenting to Rakshit (Boat stakeholder) to show two consistent campaigns with similar results rather than a single data point, especially after a prior CPC miscalculation had already created skepticism. Hassan planned to set up the call with Rakshit once final numbers (including human agent follow-up) were confirmed by Monday EOD. Kushaal overrode the suggestion for another campaign and decided to use existing data and manage any client pushback directly.

kb_faqs.question: What was the milestone around deal closures in the pilot period?
kb_faqs.answer: The team celebrated 6 annual Convin Sense deals closed within the last one month (or 7 deals from January 2026 onward including Animall). Confirmed signed deals included Health Total (Ashutosh), Bigul (Shivansh), and others. Utsav also indicated another large deal was incoming from Mrugank's accounts. The team planned an in-person celebration once Health Total and Bigul were officially signed.

kb_faqs.question: What was the silence handling issue on Ezo and how did it manifest?
kb_faqs.answer: Silence handling broke down on Ezo, causing calls to run as long as 18 minutes. The silence handling mechanism is managed from the backend and is not configurable by the implementation team. The client was already worried about their spend as a result.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Vobiz telephony issue and what was the quick fix recommended?
kb_faqs.answer: Vobiz had a call-end behavior issue causing abnormal call durations and spend discrepancies. The quick fix recommended was to switch the telephony integration to Convin telephony (Exotel). A formal issue was also raised with Vobiz to understand their call-end behavior.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Sarvam STT bug affecting Tamil language calls?
kb_faqs.answer: Sarvam STT was not transcribing customer audio for Tamil language calls, logged as ticket AC-1317. This was a P0 issue as a demo was scheduled the next day for the Tamil Nadu State Government project. The ticket was escalated to Raajit and Vaibhav for priority resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What was the bot response delay bug in AC-1266 and what was the incorrect RCA?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot was not responding for approximately 10 seconds after a customer response, causing the customer's next response to be missed. The initial RCA attributed it to the queue being cleared when the bot was interrupted, but this was incorrect. The actual issue was the bot's failure to respond in time regardless of interruption.

kb_faqs.question: What was the lead interest/outcome bug affecting Vyapar's CRM integration?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead interest was coming as NA or blank for non-connected call cases (ticket AC-1324). This was critical for Vyapar because they had a CRM push integration that consumed lead interest data directly. A ticket was raised as a priority fix.

kb_faqs.question: What was the opening statement incompleteness bug and how was it fixed?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot was generating incomplete opening statements such as 'Have 2 mins?' instead of full sentences like 'Do you have 2 minutes?' This was identified as an issue in Miles Education among other accounts. The fix was applied to the system prompt and confirmed resolved by Vaibhav.

kb_faqs.question: What was the variable substitution bug in bot call openings on 17 Feb 2026?
kb_faqs.answer: A release on the morning of 17 Feb caused variables in call opening statements to fail, resulting in bot calls starting with 'हाई, I am Taara from बोट. Am I speaking with }?' instead of the customer's name. This impacted all active campaigns including boAt and Frido. The fix required all PMs to pause AI calling and write variables as {first_name} in single brackets, not double brackets.

kb_faqs.question: What caused the call variable issue on 17 Feb and what was the permanent resolution?
kb_faqs.answer: The bug was caused by variable support being removed from static call openings in a recent release. Lovelesh added the support back and variables are now programmatically replaced rather than filled by the LLM. All PMs were instructed to use single curly braces (e.g., {first_name}) instead of double braces.

kb_faqs.question: What was the AI call billing and pulse count miscalculation bug?
kb_faqs.answer: AI call minutes and pulse count were reported as identical, and AI call cost was miscalculated in campaign billing reports, logged as ticket AC-1335. This was raised by Utsav and escalated to Raajit for resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What was the templated WhatsApp message duplication bug on Ezo?
kb_faqs.answer: Templated WhatsApp messages were being sent back-to-back repeatedly in Ezo campaigns, logged as ticket AC-1341. This was flagged to Lovelesh for resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What caused all NBA generation to stop on 19 Feb and how was it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: OpenAI billing was exhausted, causing NBA generation and AI calling to stop across all domains on 19 Feb. Durgesh made the payment to restore service. For leads that already had a failed NBA, a fresh system message had to be manually triggered to regenerate their NBA; bulk triggering could be done via internal tools.

kb_faqs.question: What was the EZO AI calling issue on 17 Feb evening and what was the root cause?
kb_faqs.answer: AI calls in EZO stopped and all campaigns were paused on the evening of 17 Feb. The agent had likely been cloned, and there is a known issue with AI cloning for agents. The workaround recommended was to create a new agent from scratch rather than using a cloned one.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Nirvasa WhatsApp outbound messaging error and its cause?
kb_faqs.answer: Outbound WhatsApp messages were not going from Nirvasa's bot number, with Meta throwing error 131042 which is related to payment. Inbound messages worked fine. The client (Aquib) had submitted tax info, but WABA payment setup was still incomplete.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Vyapar bot bug related to variable usage in calls?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot in Vyapar was using variables sent via lead ingestion even though those variables were not part of the knowledge base or script, logged as ticket AC-1394. This was escalated to Raajit and Vidhya for investigation.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Cleartrip Salesforce integration issue and what was the timeline?
kb_faqs.answer: Cleartrip needed OAuth-based Salesforce integration (ticket AC-1390) and the client granted a two-week exception before making it mandatory. Development had not yet started as of 18 Feb, with a tentative timeline of the first week of March.

kb_faqs.question: What was the NBA DND timing logic issue raised for Shaadi and how should it work?
kb_faqs.answer: When a customer did not pick up a call at 6:30 PM and the campaign DND started at 8 PM with a 14-hour cooldown, the NBA was only generated at 8:30 AM the next day instead of immediately generating a WhatsApp message before 8 PM. The expected behavior was for the system to send a WhatsApp NBA before DND begins when there is a time window available. This was logged as ticket AC-1329.

kb_faqs.question: What was the click-to-call issue in Accio Jobs and what was its impact?
kb_faqs.answer: A click-to-call issue was logged as ticket AC-1327 for Accio Jobs, causing the agent to be unable to resume calling. This was escalated to Lovelesh as the agent was actively waiting to resume calls on Convin qualified leads.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Frido bot's turn-taking issue and what was the detailed feedback?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido's client reported poor turn-taking and listening quality across multiple calls, logged as AC-1376. Detailed feedback included: latency at call start, bot switching to Hindi without the customer speaking in that language, multiple instances of poor turn-taking with unnecessary silence, and looping at the end of calls when customers kept saying thank you. Raajit confirmed turn-taking was fast in a couple of instances but was not sure if it was fully fixable.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Hindi language switching bug in Frido and what was the suspected fix?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot was switching to Hindi mid-conversation without the customer using Hindi, which was identified as a likely configuration issue. The language was set to 'Hinglish' in the campaign config, and changing this setting was flagged as a potential fix. This was part of the broader turn-taking feedback from Frido.

kb_faqs.question: What was the EZO spend discrepancy issue and how was it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: The total spend on a paused EZO campaign appeared to be continuously changing, causing client concern. The explanation was that the client had recharged their wallet and the campaign resumed mid-investigation, leading to different entry counts in different reports. The spend was confirmed correct once the campaign status was properly tracked.

kb_faqs.question: What was the lead outcome report spend discrepancy in EZO (AC-1325)?
kb_faqs.answer: A ticket (AC-1325) was raised for higher total spend appearing on leads with fewer seconds and fewer connected calls in EZO's lead outcome report. This was flagged as a data inconsistency in the billing/reporting layer.

kb_faqs.question: What was the opening line configuration bug where the defined line was not being followed?
kb_faqs.answer: The opening line defined in the Agent configuration was not being followed during calls, logged as ticket AC-1326 by Arnab. This was escalated to Raajit for investigation.

kb_faqs.question: What was the S4S API endpoint bug and how was it fixed?
kb_faqs.answer: The API endpoint for S4S Technologies domain had a trailing space which caused it to fail. Sudesha identified and removed the space, resolving the issue immediately.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Nirvasa calls stuck in 'Initiated' state issue?
kb_faqs.answer: Calls in the Nirvasa campaign were stuck in 'Initiated' state and not connecting, logged as ticket AC-1369. Shivansh was unable to receive any test calls and escalated to Lovelesh for priority resolution.

kb_faqs.question: What was the decision on Hindi language response generation for proper pronunciation?
kb_faqs.answer: Generating Hindi language responses in Devanagari script is the correct and intended behavior for proper pronunciation. This is not a bug but the expected output of the system.

kb_faqs.question: What was the EZO telephony configuration problem and what were the two options considered?
kb_faqs.answer: EZO kept switching between Vobiz and Exotel telephony, causing issues with number whitelisting. Vobiz had an unresolved call-end issue, while Exotel sometimes used a backup non-whitelisted number. The fix for Exotel's issue was to email them requesting they not use a different virtual number, making Exotel the preferred stable choice.

kb_faqs.question: What is the correct variable format for call opening statements after the 17 Feb fix?
kb_faqs.answer: Variables in call opening statements must be written in single curly braces, e.g., {first_name}, not double braces like {{first_name}}. Variables are now programmatically replaced rather than filled by the LLM, and the first bot statement is picked verbatim from the agent config with variables substituted.

kb_faqs.question: What Tier 1 pricing update was made for WhatsApp messages?
kb_faqs.answer: Marketing messages are now priced at Rs. 1, utility messages at Rs. 0.15, and free-form messages at Rs. 0.50. This replaced the previous flat rate of Rs. 0.50 for all sent messages. All cost analyses should be updated to reflect these values.

kb_faqs.question: What was the voicemail billing decision and how is it being made configurable?
kb_faqs.answer: Voicemail calls were being charged at Rs. 4 per minute by default, which the team agreed should not happen for any client. The system is being made configurable at the tenant level so voicemail billing can be enabled or disabled per client. The consensus was to turn it off by default for all clients.

kb_faqs.question: What was the configuration decision for Frido's voice selection?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido's client chose 'Pranita Pro Voice V3' from 3-4 options shared as sample recordings. Amaan (Frido SPOC) confirmed this would improve tonality. Agent handoff for coupon-related cases was also being configured in parallel.

kb_faqs.question: What was the process change for new deal onboarding introduced on 18 Feb?
kb_faqs.answer: A new handover process was introduced: Sales generates a handover form using the Sense Sales Handover GPT, then sends an email to Biswatma, Vaibhav, and Puja attaching the MSA, handover doc, and pilot deck for CSM/PM assignment. The PM owns the project through Go Live of the first use case, while the CSM shadows the engagement and owns implementation from the second use case onwards.

kb_faqs.question: What instruction was recommended to add to the 8 Pillar prompt for safety-critical situations?
kb_faqs.answer: It was recommended to add an instruction for the bot to immediately thank and disconnect the call if a customer mentions they are driving or in an emergency. Without this, the bot continues the conversation and takes time to disconnect rather than ending immediately. Product confirmed this can be added to the 8 Pillar prompt.

kb_faqs.question: What configuration change was made to address the OpenAI billing outage on 19 Feb?
kb_faqs.answer: As a workaround during the OpenAI billing outage, Biswa changed the LLM config to GPT-4.1 Mini for EZO and PW domains. After Durgesh made the billing payment, systems were restored but affected leads required a fresh system message to regenerate their NBAs.

kb_faqs.question: What was the variable mapping limitation in the current product for Shaadi.com?
kb_faqs.answer: Custom field mapping via the variable configuration screen is not available in the current product. The variables page is designed for pronunciation configuration, not API or lead intake variable mapping. The current workaround is to define variables in the prompt and let the LLM do the mapping, though this lacks precision.

kb_faqs.question: What was the ROI deck standardization instruction given to all PMs?
kb_faqs.answer: All PMs were instructed to always define what a 'pulse' means in their ROI decks, e.g., 'X 15-second pulses,' rather than just writing 'pulses' and assuming clients would understand the unit of measurement.

kb_faqs.question: What was the exotel number deactivation issue and what solution was proposed?
kb_faqs.answer: When numbers are rotated monthly, old numbers need to be deleted from Exotel to avoid Convin continuing to pay for them. There is currently no automated way to track active vs. deactivated numbers in the tenant database. Biswa proposed automating this as the current manual process risks revenue leakage.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Accio Jobs pilot metrics around agent follow-up on Convin-qualified leads?
kb_faqs.answer: Out of 90 Convin-qualified leads, the dedicated Accio agent had only attempted 6 leads since morning and had only connected with 24 out of 90 leads (connectivity of approximately 54% on attempted leads). The team identified the agent was prioritizing retries over fresh leads and was only working about 50% of qualified leads. Convin recommended giving 2 more days before presenting pilot conclusion results.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Guvi pilot metrics as of 18 Feb?
kb_faqs.answer: Guvi had 3,000 leads run in the campaign, of which 370 leads were qualified for agent follow-up. 40 counselling sessions were confirmed booked, but many leads were still in follow-up stage with agents not updating dispositions for approximately 40% (150 leads) of the qualified leads. Final data was delayed by at least 2 days.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Frido pilot context metrics around old leads?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido had approximately 40,000 leads added in December, of which around 4,000 were converted by humans and approximately 4,000 were natural self-checkouts. No agent-led outreach on old leads had been done previously, making the ROI benchmark unclear. The team decided to compare AI-generated revenue against the cost of running the campaign (target 2-3x ROI).

kb_faqs.question: What were the S4S pilot benchmark numbers for human demo conversion?
kb_faqs.answer: S4S had a field visit conversion rate of 50 demos from 100 attempted leads (50% connectivity), with 25 demos booked from 50 connected leads (50% demo rate). Of those demos, 10 out of 50 converted to sales (20% conversion). S4S was not running virtual demos before the Convin pilot.

kb_faqs.question: What were the Accio Jobs human pre-sales funnel metrics?
kb_faqs.answer: Out of 1,000 leads attempted by the human pre-sales agent, 350 were connected (35% connectivity), 70 were meaningful/hot/warm leads (20% qualification rate), and 21 were passed to the sales team (6% of connected leads). Of the 21 passed to sales, only 2.6 actually converted (approximately 12% final conversion).

kb_faqs.question: What was the Leap Scholar pilot outcome that led to a positive conclusion call?
kb_faqs.answer: The Leap pilot concluded positively with a 21% demo booking rate overall, of which 50% engaged only on WhatsApp and 50% engaged on both WhatsApp and phone. A 10.5% WhatsApp-only booking rate was noted as a strong number. The team was moving to present an annual contract and ROI analysis to the Leap team.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Leap Scholar 30% vs 53% demo booking discrepancy?
kb_faqs.answer: There was a discrepancy between a 30% demo booking number and a 53% figure that needed clarification with the Leap team SPOC Ankit before the internal ROI deck could be finalized. Shivansh met with Ankit at 2:30 PM to clarify the numbers before the internal review meeting.

kb_faqs.question: What was the EZO Books cost per lead metric?
kb_faqs.answer: The cost per lead for EZO Books was calculated as Rs. 3.86 (Rs. 15,035 total spend divided by 3,892 leads). A filter issue in the lead creation date filter was causing incorrect numbers to display in the dashboard, which was creating panic with the client (Rishikesh).

kb_faqs.question: What were the BACL pilot learnings around EMI amount and collection rate?
kb_faqs.answer: Compared to the previous month, the current BACL campaign had more cases with EMI amounts between Rs. 10,000-15,000, which was suspected to have led to a lower collection percentage. Siddhant was collating case-wise collection amount data from the BACL team for analysis to present in an internal call.

kb_faqs.question: What were the 91 Trucks pilot timeline and data collection constraints?
kb_faqs.answer: The last 91 Trucks campaign was ending that Wednesday, after which 4-5 more days were needed to receive visits booked and final visit numbers from the client. The full pilot conclusion meeting with the founder was expected to happen the following Friday or early the next week.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Handpickd pilot terminology confusion around conversions?
kb_faqs.answer: Convin was tracking 'orders placed' as conversions, but at Handpickd 'orders placed' actually means 'added to cart,' and their actual conversion event is 'orders delivered.' Of 14 reported 'orders placed' from Convin's campaign, only 4 were actually delivered. This misalignment in terminology was causing confusion in measuring the pilot's success.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Metropolis client blocker around WABA integration and how was it being handled?
kb_faqs.answer: Metropolis was blocked for over a week because the client refused to use their own card for WABA integration due to infosec concerns and also rejected using Convin's card. Philip (AM) explained available options and the client was having an internal meeting to decide an approach. Convin decided not to invest further effort until the payment method was resolved.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Kotak Life Securities integration request and Convin's response?
kb_faqs.answer: Kotak Life Securities was pushing for a Lead Intake integration, but Convin's internal assessment was that the bot flow had already been improved significantly and further improvement was unlikely. The concern was that spending one or more weeks on integration effort would be wasted if the client still found the bot quality unsatisfactory given their very high expectations. Convin decided not to proceed with the integration.

kb_faqs.question: What was the boat (Handpickd?) client's objection after the first campaign and how was it handled?
kb_faqs.answer: The client (Rakshit at boat/Handpickd) felt the results were 'a dramatic shift' and wanted one more campaign run before concluding the pilot. Since the client had remaining pilot minutes, the Convin team agreed to run one more campaign and aimed to wrap it up by Friday. The client was also asking for a revised script to experiment with improving engagement.

kb_faqs.question: What was the EZO books client's concern about dialler integration and SIM costs?
kb_faqs.answer: EZO's Rishi did not want to pay Rs. 500 per SIM for new numbers needed before dialler integration was set up. The discussion around dialler integration was pending, with Shivam tasked to close this directly with Rishi. Once Rishi aligned, Biswa and Shubham were asked to take it forward.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Bigul/SEBI-regulated client's WABA objection and how was Convin handling it?
kb_faqs.answer: The client (Bigul, a SEBI-regulated company) wanted to use their existing WABA vendor (SmartPing) instead of Convin's WABA setup, as they use WhatsApp for other marketing activities and want one channel. Convin's concern was that SmartPing lacked features like typing indicator support, and they pushed for a direct WABA with Meta. The fallback option discussed was matching SmartPing's pricing for template messages if pricing was the main objection.

kb_faqs.question: What was the S4S pilot direction decision around virtual vs. field demos?
kb_faqs.answer: S4S's original script asked for virtual demo scheduling, but S4S does not currently run virtual demos and only does field sales. Convin initially pushed back on introducing a new process (virtual demos) during the pilot as it would prevent benchmarking against existing human performance. The S4S founder ultimately confirmed he wanted the pilot to replicate the current human field visit flow, with virtual demos potentially considered post-pilot.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Frido client's feedback on bot quality and how did the team respond?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido's team flagged poor turn-taking and listening abilities as key quality concerns, citing multiple instances across calls. The team conducted an internal audit, but the client couldn't provide specific timestamps. Convin's response was to raise a product ticket (AC-1376), investigate specific call instances, change the voice to 'Pranita Pro Voice V3,' and set expectations that turn-taking is a gradual product-level improvement.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Frido client feedback about bot looping, and how did Convin audit this?
kb_faqs.answer: Frido flagged that the bot loops instead of escalating to an agent in certain scenarios. When asked for specific examples, the client could not share them. Convin conducted an internal audit and found looping was happening specifically in discount code cases, which led to adding a prompt instruction (point 7) for the bot to acknowledge and close the call in those scenarios.

kb_faqs.question: What feature request came from the Vyapar CRM integration use case?
kb_faqs.answer: Vyapar required lead interest/outcome data to be populated even for non-connected calls so their CRM could consume it directly via push integration. Currently, lead interest shows as NA or blank for these cases, requiring a product fix.

kb_faqs.question: What WhatsApp feature limitation was discovered with third-party WABA vendor Karix?
kb_faqs.answer: Karix, a third-party WABA vendor, was found to not support typing indicator functionality. This was cited as a reason to avoid integrating with clients' own WABA vendors, as Convin cannot predict what features other vendors may or may not support.

kb_faqs.question: What was the feature request for tracking daily lead ingestion in campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Shubham requested a way to track daily lead ingestion per campaign and across all campaigns, noting that the current analytics dashboard only shows attempted leads and does not surface leads that were added but not yet attempted. This was raised as a product gap.

kb_faqs.question: What was the feature request around variable support in static call openings?
kb_faqs.answer: A product requirement was identified that static call openings must also support variables like {{customer_name}}, since the issue on 17 Feb was caused by variable support being removed from static openings. Vidhya was asked to create a 1-pager explaining how variables should be written in opening statements for all PMs.

kb_faqs.question: What was the product experiment shared for data analysis and what was the feedback?
kb_faqs.answer: Raajit shared an internal tool (metchatai.lovable.app) that allows uploading raw data reports and querying insights via a chat interface using Sarvam LLM (free of cost). Initial testing by Amith found it inaccurate—when asked to find the number of hot leads, it returned 1 when the actual count was around 40. The tool was flagged as needing improvement before practical use.

kb_faqs.question: What was the feature request around Exotel number lifecycle management?
kb_faqs.answer: A need was identified to automatically delete deactivated numbers from Exotel when they are removed from a tenant, to prevent Convin from continuing to pay for unused numbers. Currently there is no active/deactivated number tracking in the tenant database, and Biswa proposed automating this process.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Cleartrip OAuth Salesforce integration requirement?
kb_faqs.answer: Cleartrip required OAuth-based Salesforce integration as a P0 requirement (ticket AC-1390). The client granted a two-week exception to the current non-OAuth integration. Product planned to start development in the first week of March.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Frido bot's turn-taking quality assessment by Raajit?
kb_faqs.answer: After listening to a specific Frido call, Raajit confirmed there were a couple of instances where turn-taking was too fast, but noted that the user's message appeared complete at the moment the bot took its turn. He was not certain if further improvement was possible and requested specific timestamps from the client's auditor to investigate further.

kb_faqs.question: What was the language switch quality issue in Miles Education and how was it addressed?
kb_faqs.answer: Miles Education's bot had issues with language switching accuracy and was generating incomplete opening statements. Vaibhav made changes to the system prompt specifically to improve language switch behavior and asked Shubham to test 20 calls to validate accuracy of language switching and completeness of opening messages. Initial testing on 5 calls showed the opening statement fix was working.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Bhanzu pilot status and why was data collection delayed?
kb_faqs.answer: Two fresh lead campaigns ran the previous day for Bhanzu, but full agent activity was not yet complete. The final enrollment numbers from the Webinar Attended campaign were also not yet shared by Lakshmi. Bhanzu planned to provide around 1,000 leads on the upcoming Saturday after a low-volume webinar, with human agents being prioritized for the lower-volume leads.

kb_faqs.question: What was learned about bot performance for Ezo Books specifically regarding voicemail calls?
kb_faqs.answer: Voicemail calls were being charged at the standard rate (Rs. 4 per minute) and adding to the client's spend. EZO's Rishikesh was panicking about reported spend figures, but the actual cost per lead was confirmed at Rs. 3.86. A filter bug in the campaign dashboard was causing inflated spend numbers to appear, adding to client concern.

kb_faqs.question: What was the Shaadi.com onboarding status including voice cloning?
kb_faqs.answer: Voice cloning was in progress for 3 voices for Shaadi.com, with Vidhya aiming to deliver at least 1 voice before a 10:30 AM client call the next day. Two voices (Richa and Vrushika) were being trained and expected to be ready by 1-2 AM, testable directly from the Sense platform.

kb_faqs.question: What were the boAt campaign (C4) conversion metrics and how did they compare to C3?
kb_faqs.answer: In C4, boAt had 41 total conversions vs 66 in C3, a 38% decrease. Cost per conversion rose 70% from 99 to 168 rupees. Qualification rate dropped from 47% to 38% of connected leads, and leads explicitly saying they disliked the product rose from 44% to 58%. Human agent follow-up on qualified leads also dropped from 31% to 24% contact rate.

kb_faqs.question: What were the boAt bot-only vs human conversion numbers in the current campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: The bot achieved 18 bot-only conversions. Human callback was done on 220 interested leads; 214 out of 237 were attempted, 54 connected, and 20 converted. Current CPC stood at 178 rupees, requiring another 30 conversions to reach the target CPC of 99. Connectivity in the campaign was 68.3%.

kb_faqs.question: What was the human conversion rate on boAt leads passed by the bot in the previous campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: Human agents converted 29 out of 255 leads passed by the bot, a conversion rate of approximately 11%. In the current campaign, human performance on qualified leads dropped to 24% contact rate, impacting overall CPC significantly.
