Best practice is to retry once after 24 hours, then move qualified leads to alternate channels (phone call). Document retry counts in reports to distinguish fresh attempts from recycled leads. Multiple retriggering reduces lead quality and conversion rates.

kb_faqs.question: How should bot performance be benchmarked against human agent performance?
kb_faqs.answer: Obtain human agent performance data from the baseline period (before bot launch) for the same lead set. Key metrics include: CTR (click-through/qualification rate), conversion rate, call duration, objection handling rate. Run bot campaigns on fresh, comparable leads over the same time period. Compare results week-over-week and adjust bot prompts based on gaps. Missing human baseline data makes accurate benchmarking impossible; this is a critical requirement before UAT.

kb_faqs.question: What KYC (Know Your Customer) drop-off use case volume can be expected?
kb_faqs.answer: In one financial services client, the KYC drop-off recovery use case yielded 4,000 leads per month. This is a high-intent segment with good conversion potential. Teams should prioritize KYC recovery as it targets warm leads with existing customer context. Expected conversion rates are typically 15-25% higher than cold outreach due to prior relationship and drop-off context.

kb_faqs.question: What is the strategy when a client hesitates on specific integrations due to approval timelines?
kb_faqs.answer: If a client says 'This will require approvals which will take too much time, we've evaluated this,' teams should accept the decision and propose alternative approaches. Push-back on unnecessary integrations and offer simpler alternatives (e.g., PSTN transfer instead of full dialer integration, CRM webhook instead of reverse feed). Focus on what's feasible within their timeline and can still deliver value. Don't force integrations that create friction.

kb_faqs.question: How should teams handle situations where multiple dialer options are under evaluation?
kb_faqs.answer: When clients are evaluating multiple integration options (e.g., Vobiz vs. Elision), provide clear technical documentation outlining dev effort required from the client's side for each option. Present trade-offs transparently (stability, complexity, cost) and let clients choose based on their dev capacity. Avoid changing recommendations mid-evaluation as it damages credibility. Create a single, updated document comparing all options rather than separate specs.

kb_faqs.question: What is the recommended approach when stakeholder alignment is lost on integration direction?
kb_faqs.answer: When internal Convin teams lose alignment (e.g., after originally closing a conversation), address it transparently with the client. Don't proceed with a U-turn without explanation. Schedule a call with the client to acknowledge the shift, explain the reason (e.g., stability concerns), and propose the revised approach. This preserves trust better than quietly changing direction. If unsure about stability, say so—clients prefer transparency over false confidence.

kb_faqs.question: What information is needed before scheduling a client kick-off call?
kb_faqs.answer: Before the kick-off, confirm: (1) client's main use case and campaign objective; (2) lead volume and source; (3) required integrations (CRM, dialer, WABA); (4) human handoff process; (5) success metrics. Send an agenda 24 hours in advance. During the call, address any technical or process questions and agree on UAT timeline. Document all action items and owners. A clear kick-off prevents scope creep and integration surprises later.

kb_faqs.question: How should teams coordinate between multiple internal teams (Sales, CSM, Product, Integrations) before client engagement?
kb_faqs.answer: Hold an internal kick-off with Sales, CSM, Product, and Integrations present to align on: (1) integration approach and timeline; (2) resource allocation (who owns which task); (3) known blockers; (4) client communication strategy. Create a shared document outlining deliverables and dates. Use a project tracker (Jira, Asana) to monitor progress and escalate blockers immediately. Misalignment between internal teams is the #1 cause of client delays.

kb_faqs.question: What happens when the Knowledge Base and Agent Training sections fall out of sync?
kb_faqs.answer: When KB content is updated but Agent Training is not (or vice versa), the bot may use outdated information or mixed versions, leading to inconsistent responses. A hard refresh should auto-sync, but this doesn't always work. Best practice is to update both sections simultaneously and verify sync before campaign launch. If sync fails, file a bug ticket and delay campaign until resolved. This is a critical consistency check.

kb_faqs.question: How should variable naming conventions be standardized across prompts and CSV uploads?
kb_faqs.answer: Use consistent casing throughout: choose either {Product_1} or {product_1}, not both. Define a naming convention document and share with all PMs/CSMs. When downloading the bulk upload CSV template, it will auto-populate variable columns in the correct format. Mismatched variable names (case-sensitive) cause upload failures and are tedious to debug. Amend variable naming standards to the pre-campaign checklist.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What constitutes a 'bad' lead CSV and what should teams check before upload?
kb_faqs.answer: A bad CSV typically has: (1) missing or extra columns compared to the template; (2) mismatched variable names (case sensitivity issues); (3) blank values in Mandatory fields; (4) invalid data types (e.g., text in a number field); (5) special characters breaking delimiters. Teams should validate CSVs row-by-row before uploading, use the fresh template, and test on a small batch (10-50 rows) first. Bad CSVs cause upload failures and campaign delays.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What is the difference between a 'good' and 'bad' lead list in terms of outcomes?
kb_faqs.answer: A good lead list has: (1) accurate phone numbers; (2) recent, opt-in contacts; (3) relevant to the use case; (4) low DND overlap. These convert at 15-30% depending on vertical. A bad list has: (1) outdated or duplicate numbers; (2) high DND percentage; (3) cold/random contacts; (4) wrong vertical fit. These convert at <5%. Lead quality is more important than volume—1000 good leads outperform 10,000 bad leads. Advise clients to invest in data quality.

kb_faqs.question: What is the process for marking leads as 'not evaluated' vs. 'cold' in qualification reporting?
kb_faqs.answer: 'Not Evaluated' means the bot did not connect with the lead (no interaction). 'Cold' means the bot connected but the prospect did not show intent/qualification. These are different outcomes. Teams should ensure that audit rules reflect this distinction: leads with zero interaction should be marked 'Not Evaluated' (or 'Not Connected'), while connected calls with negative sentiment should be 'Cold.' This distinction is important for lead scoring and handoff prioritization.

kb_faqs.question: When should teams involve product/tech during campaign setup vs. post-launch?
kb_faqs.answer: Involve Product/Tech during setup for: (1) custom variable mapping; (2) CRM integration configuration; (3) webhook payload design; (4) dialer integration testing. Post-launch, tech should only address bugs and critical issues. Preventive involvement during UAT reduces go-live risk by 60%. Have a tech sync before UAT starts. This shifts left and avoids launch-day surprises.

kb_faqs.question: What should teams do when a product release introduces a breaking change (e.g., variable management)?
kb_faqs.answer: Immediately communicate the change to all CSMs/PMs with: (1) detailed impact (which workflows break); (2) workaround steps; (3) remediation deadline; (4) examples. Create a runbook for clients. Offer sync calls to walk through the change. For impacted live campaigns, consider rolling back the release or hotfixing. This is a high-priority comms issue—clients should hear from Convin first, not discover it themselves. Document the lesson learned.

kb_faqs.question: How should report download issues be prioritized and escalated?
kb_faqs.answer: Report downloads that are stuck in 'Queued' status for >30 minutes are critical issues affecting client reporting and should be escalated immediately to tech with: (1) org name; (2) campaign name; (3) report type; (4) time initiated. These often indicate backend queue overflow or database locks. Temporary workaround: try downloading a smaller date range or different report type. Root cause should be investigated post-incident. Set up monitoring for report queue times.

When bot-qualified leads are not being followed up by human agents promptly, it indicates a handoff SLA breach. Teams should: (1) confirm CRM/lead sync is working; (2) verify human agent queue is being monitored; (3) check if lead priority is set correctly; (4) call the client to reinforce handoff process. Delays >24 hours suggest process breakdown, not technical issue. Establish a daily standup to monitor handoff metrics.
kb_faqs.question: How should teams monitor and enforce lead handoff SLAs?
What questions should be asked before committing to a specific integration approach?
kb_faqs.answer: Ask: (1) Do you have dev bandwidth to implement on your end? (2) Have you evaluated alternatives? (3) What is your timeline for approval? (4) Will this integrate with your existing systems? (5) Do you have budget allocated? These questions reveal hidden blockers before teams invest effort. If answers are unclear, schedule an internal sync before responding to the client. Don't over-promise on complex integrations.

kb_faqs.question: What is the 8-pillar agent prompt creation framework and when should it be used?
kb_faqs.answer: The 8-pillar framework is a structured approach to designing bot prompts covering: (1) tone/personality; (2) listening/understanding; (3) response; (4) handling objections; (5) discovery questions; (6) qualification criteria; (7) call-to-action; (8) edge cases. This is now available in the Agent Training tab during agent configuration. Teams should use this framework for all new campaigns to ensure comprehensive prompt design. It replaces ad-hoc KB linking.

kb_faqs.question: How should teams handle feature requests that are low-priority or require significant dev effort?
kb_faqs.answer: Acknowledge the request, explain the effort/timeline required, and offer a workaround or alternative if available. Don't defer indefinitely—give a clear answer (Yes/No/Maybe + timeline). If deferred, add to the Product roadmap and communicate the expected timeline. For critical-path features blocking go-live, escalate to leadership. Low-priority features should not hold up campaign launch; proceed and address post-launch.

kb_faqs.question: What should be included in a sales handover document when transitioning from Sales to CSM/Integrations?
kb_faqs.answer: The handover should include: (1) client background and decision-makers; (2) use case and success criteria; (3) technical requirements and integrations; (4) timeline and milestones; (5) known constraints/blockers; (6) budget and contract terms; (7) escalation contacts. This document prevents information loss during team transitions. Schedule a sync between Sales and CSM to walk through the handover. Missing details cause re-work and delays during implementation.

kb_faqs.question: When should UAT campaign results be reviewed and what metrics should be assessed?
kb_faqs.answer: Review UAT results before scaling to production campaigns. Key metrics: (1) call completion rate (% calls that connected); (2) qualification rate (% of completions that qualified); (3) bot quality score (agent feedback); (4) dialogue flow (any drops/errors); (5) prompt effectiveness (objection handling). If any metric is <70%, address root cause before full launch. UAT should take 3-5 days minimum with 100-500 leads to capture statistically valid data.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: What is the purpose of the NBA (Next Best Action) loop and how should it be configured?
kb_faqs.answer: The NBA loop determines the sequence and conditions for retrying failed leads across channels (phone, WhatsApp, SMS). Configuration should include: (1) retry attempts per channel; (2) time delays between retries; (3) DND hour exclusion; (4) channel fallback logic. Common issue: NBA rules not being respected due to bugs (disabling not working, DND hours not honored). Test NBA logic extensively in UAT to catch these issues before live campaigns.

kb_faqs.question: What should teams do when connectivity rates are lower than expected?
kb_faqs.answer: Investigate in this order: (1) Are phone numbers valid/accurate? (2) Is DND filtering working? (3) Are we calling during business hours? (4) Is the dialer integration working correctly? (5) Are there network issues on the client's end? Common causes: bad lead data (invalid numbers), high DND overlap, calling at wrong times, dialer misconfiguration. Request call logs and audio samples from the client. Compare against their historical connectivity rates with human agents.

kb_faqs.question: What cost-per-acquisition (CPA) benefits can clients expect from bot outreach?
kb_faqs.answer: No specific CPA figures are documented in the conversations, but context suggests bot CPA is significantly lower than human agent CPA due to scale. ROI is typically positive after 500-1000 qualified leads depending on vertical. Key drivers: (1) lead quality; (2) conversion rate; (3) call completion rate; (4) bot usage cost (Convin fees + dialer fees). Clients should measure ROI weekly and adjust targeting if CPA exceeds acceptable thresholds.

kb_faqs.question: How frequently should campaign performance be reviewed and communicated to clients?
kb_faqs.answer: Establish a weekly cadence for performance reviews (every Monday/Friday) with the client. Share: (1) leads processed; (2) qualification rate; (3) conversion rate; (4) any issues/blockers; (5) actions for next week. Use visual dashboards when possible. If performance is below target, propose prompt adjustments or lead quality changes. This weekly rhythm keeps clients engaged and catches issues early. Monthly reviews are too infrequent for optimization.

kb_faqs.question: What are the most common reasons for campaign go-live delays?
kb_faqs.answer: Top 5 reasons: (1) WABA integration approval (2-3 days); (2) client-side integrations (dialer, CRM) not ready; (3) lead CSV validation issues; (4) approvals within client organization; (5) bot prompt finalization. Most delays are client-side (60-70%) rather than Convin-side (30-40%). To mitigate: confirm all blockers in the kickoff, establish clear deadlines, escalate delays >1 day immediately, and offer alternatives if primary path is blocked.

kb_faqs.question: What is the typical timeline for uploading and processing large lead batches?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead uploads of 7,000 records should be processed within 3–4 hours. However, delays can occur due to worker pod restarts or integration issues, causing uploads to fail or get stuck in processing queues. When issues arise, they must be resolved immediately to maintain client trust and ensure campaigns can launch on schedule. Always monitor upload progress in real-time and communicate ETAs clearly to clients.

kb_faqs.question: What causes lead upload failures and how should they be handled?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead upload failures are typically caused by worker pod restarts, which push tasks to failed queues and prevent record processing. When this occurs, execute a full re-run of the failed batch with flush-and-fill logic to avoid duplicates with previously uploaded records. Release a permanent fix to prevent recurrence. Communicate transparently with the client about the issue, the resolution timeline, and impact to campaign launch.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: How should lead data quality be validated before campaign launch?
kb_faqs.answer: Before uploading leads, verify that the CSV format matches the expected schema and that lead counts are accurate. After upload, cross-check the number of records loaded against the number supplied by the client—any discrepancies (e.g., 7,000 sent but only 6,999 loaded) should be investigated immediately. Check for duplicate entries and ensure all leads are in the Active stage and ready for campaign execution.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: What is the default NBA (Next Best Action) execution behavior after lead upload?
kb_faqs.answer: After lead upload completes successfully, NBAs (Next Best Actions) are automatically scheduled and executed according to DND (Do Not Disturb) configuration and retry windows. Calling typically resumes the next day. Default retry limit is 5 attempts per lead, but this can be increased if needed to improve connectivity rates. Monitor NBA execution logs to ensure calls are being initiated on schedule.

kb_faqs.question: What connectivity rates are typically achieved by AI bots versus human agents?
kb_faqs.answer: AI bots typically achieve ~80% daily connectivity, while human agents operate at ~90–93%. To close this gap, increasing retry attempts from the default 5 to higher limits can improve connectivity. Each additional retry should be tested thoroughly before deployment. Connectivity improvements are critical for scaling campaigns and justifying bot performance against human benchmarks.

kb_faqs.question: What are the best practices for opening lines in AI voice bot scripts?
kb_faqs.answer: Avoid generic opening lines like 'Namaste XYZ, this is Neha calling from ABC. Is this a good time to talk?' Instead, always add context upfront: 'Namaste XYZ, this is Neha calling from ABC. I'm calling because you showed interest/inquired about PQR. Is this a good time to talk?' Adding context in the opening line significantly improves engagement and reduces early call drop-offs, especially in large campaigns (500–1000 leads). This small change can materially impact conversion rates.

kb_faqs.question: How should naturalness prompts be deployed in Convin Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: Naturalness prompts are language-specific and differ for Hinglish/Hindi versus English interactions. Replace Pillar 8 instructions entirely with the appropriate naturalness prompt and delete Pillar 4 completely. Do not rename remaining pillars. The Hindi/Hinglish and English naturalness prompts cannot be used together in the same campaign. Test thoroughly before deploying to live leads. Hinglish prompts help reduce unnaturalness in regional language campaigns while English prompts improve conversational fluency.

kb_faqs.question: What are the default LLM temperature, TTS speed, and volume settings for AI agents?
kb_faqs.answer: For demo/high-flexibility domains: temperature 0.7, speed 1.0, volume 1.0–1.3. For production domains with detailed prompts: temperature 0.2–0.4 (lower for better prompt adherence), speed 1.0, volume 1.0. Temperature controls output variability—lower values increase consistency and adherence to the prompt, while higher values allow more flexibility. Adjust speed and volume by voice type and use case requirements.

kb_faqs.question: What objection do clients raise about WhatsApp integration and how should it be handled?
kb_faqs.answer: Clients often require a verified WhatsApp Business Account (WABA) to send messages through the channel. If they don't have one, they may cite lengthy vendor approval timelines (weeks or months). The recommended approach is to start the pilot with AI calling only and run WhatsApp in parallel once WABA verification is complete. This avoids delaying campaign launch and allows the bot to prove performance on voice before adding messaging.

kb_faqs.question: What is the new WABA integration process and how does it improve payment handling?
kb_faqs.answer: The new WABA integration now supports credit line instead of requiring credit card information upfront. This eliminates payment failures and simplifies onboarding. During WABA integration setup, enable credit line (PMs will receive step-by-step guidance). For existing WABA integrations using credit cards, migrate them to credit line via a dedicated migration session. This change makes WABA integrations more reliable and reduces operational friction.

kb_faqs.question: Do clients have the ability to bulk download recordings and transcripts from campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Bulk download is not currently available on the Convin platform due to data security and workload management concerns. Instead, transcripts and recordings will be made available via APIs, allowing clients to download them to their own systems at no additional cost. This feature is expected within 2 weeks. In the interim, clients can export individual recordings/transcripts manually from the dashboard.

kb_faqs.question: What issues have been observed with lead interest classification accuracy?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead interest classification accuracy has degraded in recent weeks, dropping from ~85% in early campaign phases to lower levels. Issues include leads being misclassified as 'Not Evaluated' despite connected calls >5 seconds, and Hot/Warm classifications changing unexpectedly to Cold on re-run. Root cause is under investigation but may be linked to recent product releases. This impacts deal metrics significantly and requires immediate resolution before scaling campaigns further.

kb_faqs.question: What causes 'lead already exists' errors during bulk lead uploads?
kb_faqs.answer: This is a known issue related to worker pod restarts disrupting progress tracking during uploads. Leads get flagged with 'lead already exists' errors in the error file but are still added to the campaign in Active stage, causing duplicate records and inflated lead counts. A permanent fix is under development. For now, manually reconcile duplicates and ensure error logs are reviewed after every large upload.

kb_faqs.question: How should human agent costs be estimated for ROI calculations?
kb_faqs.answer: Do not calculate human agent cost based on salary alone. Include device costs, training, audits, infrastructure, and management overhead. Even agents with salaries <₹20K should be budgeted at ₹35K–₹40K total cost per agent per month for realistic ROI comparisons. This ensures fair benchmarking between AI bot and human agent performance and avoids overstating bot savings.

kb_faqs.question: What conversion rate should be expected from AI voice bots in early-stage campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Early conversion rates are typically 1–1.3% in UAT phases. As the bot learns from more interactions and client purchase data is integrated, conversion rates are expected to improve significantly. Target benchmarks vary by use case but should be established based on the client's internal human agent performance (if available) or industry standards. Continuous optimization of prompts and call scripts will improve conversion over time.

kb_faqs.question: What TAT expectations apply to lead handoffs from bot to human agents?
kb_faqs.answer: When bots hand off qualified leads to human agents, handoff must occur within committed SLA windows. Delays in handoff breach client trust and impact conversion. Leads should be tagged with bot-generated qualification data (Hot/Warm/Cold) and conversation context so agents can follow up efficiently. Tracking demos booked, attended, and deals closed from bot-handed leads is essential for measuring overall campaign ROI.

kb_faqs.question: What pre-campaign checklist items should be completed before launching a Sense campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: Before launch: (1) Verify lead CSV schema and record count; (2) Complete UAT with representative lead sample (50–100 records); (3) Confirm wallet/credit top-up is sufficient for planned call volume; (4) Validate DND hours and NBA retry windows with client; (5) Confirm bot persona, voice, and opening script with stakeholders; (6) Test all integration touchpoints (WhatsApp, CRM, reporting); (7) Ensure team is trained on monitoring and escalation procedures. Any gaps will delay launch.

kb_faqs.question: What is the process for managing stakeholder transitions during client engagements?
kb_faqs.answer: When a key stakeholder leaves (e.g., a project champion or decision-maker), immediately identify and connect with their replacement. Ensure the incoming stakeholder is brought up to speed on project history, decisions made, and current blockers. Formalize this transition through handover documents and sync meetings. Without proper stakeholder continuity, project momentum stalls, approvals are delayed, and trust erodes.

kb_faqs.question: What causes platform slowness and how is it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: Dashboard slowness has been observed during periods with 280+ concurrent campaigns. Root cause is typically database query performance or infrastructure load. Optimization fixes include query optimization, caching, and infrastructure scaling. Hard-refresh browsers after fixes are deployed. If slowness persists, report with specific examples and request priority attention from the tech team.

kb_faqs.question: What specific misclassifications have been identified in lead interest tagging?
kb_faqs.answer: Examples include: (1) Leads with connected calls >5 seconds being tagged 'Not Evaluated' instead of Hot/Warm/Cold; (2) Hot/Warm leads being re-evaluated and downgraded to Cold without clear reason; (3) Inconsistent classifications across similar lead profiles. These issues appear concentrated in recent campaigns and are distinct from initial phases when accuracy was ~85%. Root cause investigation is ongoing and resolution is critical before scaling.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: Why should retry limits be increased from the default 5 attempts?
kb_faqs.answer: Default 5 retries achieve ~80% connectivity. To match human agent performance (~90–93%), increasing retry limits to 6–7+ attempts should be tested. This is especially important for leads that failed to connect on initial attempts due to network issues, DND hours, or timing. Each additional retry should be tested in a pilot before full deployment to ensure quality and cost-effectiveness.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What data quality issues should be caught during CSV validation?
kb_faqs.answer: Check for: (1) Missing or incorrect phone number format; (2) Duplicate entries across uploads; (3) Leads already in system flagged correctly; (4) Data type mismatches (e.g., text in numeric fields); (5) DND/privacy restrictions already applied; (6) Lead source and segment accurately captured. Work with clients to define acceptable error thresholds before upload. High-quality input data directly improves bot performance and conversation outcomes.

kb_faqs.question: How many UAT leads should be tested before scaling to full campaign?
kb_faqs.answer: Start with 50–100 lead samples to validate bot behavior, prompt effectiveness, and call quality. Review transcripts and recordings from UAT to identify script improvements and address any hallucinations or misunderstandings. Once UAT conversion and engagement metrics meet targets, scale incrementally (e.g., 500 leads) before full production rollout. This approach reduces risk and identifies issues early.

kb_faqs.question: What male voice options are recommended for AI bots?
kb_faqs.answer: Based on testing, 'Sahil' has shown good performance in financial services use cases. 'Rahul' is also available as a default Cartesia voice option. Voice selection should match the bot persona and use case—professional tones work better for B2B/financial services, while friendly tones suit consumer verticals. Test voice options with UAT leads and gather feedback before finalizing for production.

kb_faqs.question: What issue occurs with rule-based tools like Terminal Tool not executing?
kb_faqs.answer: In some cases, rule-based tools stop executing mid-campaign despite working in earlier phases. Example: Terminal Tool in a campaign stops being called. Root cause is typically a configuration issue or a backend service disruption. Debug by providing: tenant name, tool name, and agent name for testing. Escalate to product engineering with detailed reproduction steps. This impacts lead routing and downstream conversion.

kb_faqs.question: What is the workaround for increasing retry limits in Non-NBA flow campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: A product ticket should be raised to increase retry limits in Non-NBA flow. This feature is not available by default but can be prioritized if blocking campaign launch. The fix will apply globally to all campaigns using Non-NBA flow, so test impacts before deploying widely. Once released, retry limits can be configured per campaign.

kb_faqs.question: What investigation approach should be used when lead counts don't match between client and Convin systems?
kb_faqs.answer: When a client reports sending 3,000 leads but only 1,600 appear in the system: (1) Check integration logs and API response codes on the client's CRM system (Convin has limited visibility if they're using CRM-based APIs); (2) Request client IT to validate their API calls succeeded without errors; (3) Cross-check the last date leads were received (e.g., leads sent for entire March vs. only through February); (4) Review any error messages in failed upload queues. This must be resolved before reporting on conversion metrics.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: How are call slots distributed when multiple campaigns are running on the same domain?
kb_faqs.answer: Call slots are distributed in round-robin fashion by default. If a user has reserved slots for a specific campaign, those slots are not used by other campaigns—they remain idle if that campaign has low lead volume. This can cause other campaigns to queue. To optimize, use default round-robin allocation unless specific campaign prioritization is needed. Communicate slot requirements and projections upfront.

kb_faqs.question: Should sales cycles impact campaign duration and lead volume planning?
kb_faqs.answer: Yes. Longer sales cycles (30–45 days) require longer pilot periods to measure true conversion. If a client won't share historical performance data, establish conservative baselines and plan for extended observation periods. Track demos booked, attended, and deals closed separately from initial qualification to measure full funnel performance.

kb_faqs.question: How should campaign scaling strategy be communicated to clients?
kb_faqs.answer: Start with small batches (500 leads) to validate bot performance and gather feedback before scaling to larger batches (1,000–5,000 leads). Communicate that this staged approach reduces risk and improves quality. Set clear gates: UAT → 500 leads pilot → analysis → 1,000 leads → full scale. This builds confidence and ensures the client sees incremental improvement in metrics before major commitment.

kb_faqs.question: What is the recommended daily lead volume for steady-state campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: 100 leads per day is a sustainable steady-state volume for initial pilots. This allows for detailed monitoring and optimization without overwhelming infrastructure. For larger volumes (e.g., 5,000 leads daily), significantly more dialler channels are required (30+) and additional infrastructure planning is necessary. Always confirm that lead volume projections align with available calling capacity before launching.

kb_faqs.question: What happens when campaign metrics don't clone properly when campaigns are duplicated?
kb_faqs.answer: Campaign cloning may fail to properly copy metrics and configuration settings to the new campaign. This creates discrepancies between the source and cloned campaign, impacting reporting. A product ticket should be raised to fix the cloning logic. In the interim, manually verify all metrics and settings in cloned campaigns before launching.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: How should lead data be organized to support proper sequencing and DND compliance?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead CSVs should include: phone number, lead source, opt-in status, DND flags, segment, and any custom attributes. Ensure DND compliance is pre-applied (e.g., leads on DND list are marked). This prevents wasted dial attempts and regulatory issues. Work with the client to define field mappings and validation rules before first upload.

kb_faqs.question: When should campaigns be paused pending issue resolution?
kb_faqs.answer: Pause campaigns when: (1) Lead interest classification accuracy drops significantly; (2) Critical product bugs are discovered (e.g., NBA not executing, tool failures); (3) Client data integrity issues are found (e.g., duplicate leads, missing records). Resume only after the issue is resolved and tested. This prevents wasted lead spend and maintains data quality. Communicate pause reasons and resolution timeline to clients.

kb_faqs.question: What information should be included in bot-to-human handoff data?
kb_faqs.answer: Include: (1) Lead qualification (Hot/Warm/Cold); (2) Call transcript or summary of key discussion points; (3) Bot confidence score in classification; (4) Next best action recommended; (5) Conversation duration and engagement level; (6) Any objections or concerns raised. This enables human agents to continue the conversation seamlessly and follow up effectively.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What are the risks of not validating lead count accuracy before campaign launch?
kb_faqs.answer: If uploaded lead count doesn't match source count, you risk: (1) Incorrect ROI calculations (e.g., if 1,000 leads were lost, true CPA is higher); (2) Underreporting performance (metrics based on incomplete data set); (3) Client dissatisfaction and trust loss; (4) Hidden data quality issues that affect bot performance. Always reconcile lead counts and investigate discrepancies before considering a campaign ready for analysis.

kb_faqs.question: What is the structure of an effective bot system prompt?
kb_faqs.answer: Effective prompts follow a pillar-based structure: Pillar 1–3: Bot persona and context; Pillar 4: Deleted when deploying naturalness prompts; Pillar 5–7: Conversation flow and objection handling; Pillar 8: Replaced with language-specific naturalness prompt (Hinglish or English). Keep prompts concise and actionable. Test thoroughly before deployment. Document prompt versions with dates/v-numbers to track changes.

kb_faqs.question: How should lead interest re-evaluation be approached when accuracy is questioned?
kb_faqs.answer: If lead interest classifications are inconsistent, re-run interest evaluation on a sample of flagged leads and compare results. If classifications change significantly on re-run (e.g., Hot → Cold), this indicates a systemic issue. Conduct RCA with product/data teams to identify root cause (e.g., model drift, feature changes, data quality). Don't proceed with campaigns until root cause is identified and fixed.

kb_faqs.question: What monitoring approach should be used when connectivity is below target?
kb_faqs.answer: If daily connectivity drops below ~80%, investigate: (1) DND hour configuration (are leads being called during restricted hours?); (2) Retry timing and intervals; (3) Lead data quality (invalid numbers, wrong formats); (4) Network/infrastructure issues; (5) Call handling rules. Run a small test batch (100 leads) with diagnostics enabled to identify the bottleneck. Once resolved, scale back up.

kb_faqs.question: What should be confirmed with the client regarding DND and retry configuration?
kb_faqs.answer: Confirm: (1) DND hours (e.g., no calls 9 PM–9 AM); (2) Days to exclude (weekends, holidays); (3) Retry timing (e.g., retry after 24 hours, max 5 attempts); (4) Lead prioritization (FIFO vs. other methods); (5) Escalation rules (which leads go to human agents). Document these in writing and build them into campaign configuration before launch. Any misalignment will cause performance issues.

kb_faqs.question: What causes NBA scheduling or execution failures?
kb_faqs.answer: NBA failures can occur due to: (1) Worker pod restarts interrupting the process; (2) Configuration errors in DND/retry rules; (3) Campaign not being in Active state; (4) Integration failures preventing lead import. When NBA generation shows 0 attempts for leads, check: NBA logs for errors, lead upload status, campaign configuration, and backend service health. Restart NBA generation if a transient issue is detected.

kb_faqs.question: What is the recommended maximum call duration for AI voice bot campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: The maximum call duration should be set to 1000 seconds (approximately 16.7 minutes), which is higher than the default of 300 seconds. This allows for more comprehensive conversations during demos and live campaigns. However, call auto-drop occurs in demos if this is not properly configured, so ensure it is set during campaign setup.

kb_faqs.question: How does word replacement in the bot configuration affect pronunciation quality?
kb_faqs.answer: Word replacements (such as 'Par' to 'पार') can negatively impact pronunciation when they are applied across the entire conversation context. If a word replacement is set for greeting purposes but gets applied to other contexts (like 'saath hi' instead of 'saath hei'), it creates mispronunciation issues. The workaround is to remove problematic word replacements from the domain configuration, especially when they interfere with natural conversation flow.

kb_faqs.question: Should I use Temperature or Reasoning model in LLM configuration for Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: The recommended approach is to use the Reasoning model rather than Temperature-based configuration for more consistent and higher-quality LLM responses. The Reasoning model provides better control over bot behavior and conversation quality compared to adjusting temperature parameters.

kb_faqs.question: What is the root cause of Meta OTP errors showing 'incorrect' with 0 attempts left?
kb_faqs.answer: Meta sometimes shows OTP as incorrect even when the correct code is entered, leaving 0 attempts remaining. This is a Meta platform issue, not a Convin Sense issue. The workaround is to migrate from credit card payment methods to credit line (mCarbon) for Meta WABA setup, as credit lines can be added without OTP verification and avoid this repeated coordination problem.

kb_faqs.question: What pre-campaign checklist items are critical before go-live?
kb_faqs.answer: Critical pre-campaign items include: (1) UAT campaign execution and sign-off, (2) wallet balance verification and top-up with approval, (3) CSV lead data validation with proper formatting, (4) configuration of call scripts and DND settings, (5) WhatsApp WABA setup with proper payment methods, (6) testing of tool definitions and prompts, and (7) internal team training on the campaign. Skipping any of these can delay campaign launch or cause performance issues.

kb_faqs.question: What causes WhatsApp message delivery failures with error 131402?
kb_faqs.answer: Error 131402 from Meta indicates issues with WABA account configuration, typically related to payment method and tax information not being properly set up. Additionally, if the same phone number is being used in multiple WABAs simultaneously, it causes certificate generation and integration failures. The fix is to ensure each number is registered in only one WABA and verify payment/tax details are complete on the Meta Business Account.

kb_faqs.question: How do clients react to low conversion rates from AI-only calling campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Clients experiencing 1-2% conversion rates with AI-only campaigns often question whether continuing with an AI agent makes sense. The recommended response is to introduce a human-in-the-loop approach where warm/hot leads are handed off to human agents for closure. This typically shows measurable uplift and addresses client concerns about AI limitations in driving conversions.

kb_faqs.question: What is a typical lead calling cadence that clients follow?
kb_faqs.answer: Many clients follow a structured calling cadence where a set of customers is contacted on Day 1, Day 7, and Day 14 after initial interaction. This means if a customer set has already been called on Day 1, they cannot be called again on Day 5 or other intermediate days. This cadence must be respected in campaign scheduling to align with client internal processes and avoid duplicate contact attempts.

kb_faqs.question: Is it mandatory to disclose that the caller is an AI agent in the call opening?
kb_faqs.answer: Many clients now require that calls explicitly state 'This is an AI Agent' in the opening greeting. If this disclosure is mandatory, it conflicts with the 'Naturalness' prompt settings that aim for conversational authenticity. The recommendation is to honor client compliance requirements and remove or adjust Naturalness settings when explicit AI disclosure is mandated.

kb_faqs.question: What is the typical TAT expectation for lead handoff from AI bot to human agents?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead handoff TAT varies by client, but delays in human agent response have been observed within 24-48 hours of bot qualification. Clients may not have adequate human resources allocated for follow-up, or agents may deprioritize leads if they appear low-quality. Setting clear expectations during kickoff and ensuring human agent training on lead context from AI calls is critical to prevent funnel drop.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What makes a good vs. bad lead CSV for campaign upload?
kb_faqs.answer: Good CSVs have: (1) proper name formatting (title case, not ALL CAPS), (2) clean phone numbers without extra formatting, (3) accurate metadata fields, (4) no duplicate entries, and (5) fields that match the campaign schema. Bad CSVs have CAPS-only names (e.g., 'BAJAJ PULSAR'), missing required fields, or inconsistent formatting. Poor CSV quality leads to pronunciation errors, failed contact attempts, and incorrect lead scoring.

kb_faqs.question: Why are leads being incorrectly classified as Hot/Warm when they should be Cold?
kb_faqs.answer: Misclassification often occurs when CRM metadata and lead summary fields are passed to the LLM evaluation model. For example, if pincode is already in CRM metadata, the LLM assumes it was explicitly provided by the customer during the call, creating false positives. Similarly, 'proposed time' in the lead summary gets interpreted as a confirmed booking. The fix is to remove CRM metadata and lead summary from LLM inputs and use only data explicitly mentioned during the conversation.

kb_faqs.question: What causes text normalization problems affecting word pronunciation in calls?
kb_faqs.answer: Certain domain-specific terms (like 'Bajaj', 'Platina') get mispronounced due to text normalization issues in the LLM or TTS layer. This is especially problematic when lead names are in ALL CAPS format. The issue requires listening to actual call recordings to identify and involves either fixing the normalization rules or requesting clients to provide clean data. This is not just a prompt issue but may require engineering-level fixes.

kb_faqs.question: What is the impact of setting WhatsApp message limit to 5 without customer response?
kb_faqs.answer: Setting the maximum number of WhatsApp messages to 5 without customer response significantly degrades WhatsApp health and deliverability. The recommended practice is to always start with 3 messages maximum, and if health metrics decline, reduce to 2 or even 1. The default system value should be changed from 5 to 3 to prevent campaigns from inadvertently hitting WhatsApp rate limits.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: When should NBA (Next Best Action) campaigns be triggered for follow-up?
kb_faqs.answer: NBA campaigns should be triggered only when specific lead-qualifying events occur (e.g., customer explicitly asks for details via WhatsApp). If a customer doesn't request information, an NBA should not be auto-generated. Issues arise when NBA logic doesn't properly check for customer intent before generating follow-up templates. Always validate that the trigger condition was actually met before issuing an NBA.

kb_faqs.question: Why are WhatsApp messages being sent in Hinglish when the campaign language is set to English?
kb_faqs.answer: This occurs when a customer has an existing conversation history on WhatsApp with the business number. In such cases, the system sends free-form (non-templated) messages instead of template messages, and the LLM may switch to Hinglish based on the conversation history or language patterns. The workaround is to check if the number has prior chat history; if so, expect non-templated messages. For new numbers, ensure templates are pre-approved in the correct language.

kb_faqs.question: How do DND (Do Not Disturb) settings at campaign level affect call connectivity?
kb_faqs.answer: When DND is misconfigured at the campaign level, it can prevent both AI and human click-to-call attempts from connecting, even if the leads were previously reachable. The system may show leads as DND blocked for human agents, but AI calls still connect because they bypass certain DND checks. The fix is to verify DND configuration is accurate and applies only to intended lead segments, not entire campaigns.

kb_faqs.question: What are the key pillar components in the Naturalness prompt for conversational quality?
kb_faqs.answer: The Naturalness prompt includes multiple pillars (at least 8 identified). Pillar 4 and Pillar 8 are specific focus areas for conversation flow. When these pillars are removed or changed, the bot's behavior can shift—for example, asking many questions in a monologue rather than maintaining conversational back-and-forth. Changes to naturalness settings should be tested on sample calls before full campaign deployment.

kb_faqs.question: What causes dashboard metric numbers to mismatch with actual call counts?
kb_faqs.answer: Mismatches between dashboard figures and actual counts indicate a backend data consistency issue. This was observed as a product bug (ticket AC-1867) and has been fixed in subsequent releases. During pilots, if metrics appear incorrect, report it immediately with specific lead examples so the data reconciliation can be checked and corrected.

kb_faqs.question: What causes Hinglish LLM generation of 'hi' instead of 'hei' in conversation?
kb_faqs.answer: This occurs due to greedy word replacement logic that was introduced to improve greeting handling but gets applied across the entire conversation. For example, 'saath hi' gets replaced to 'saath हाई' which affects pronunciation in non-greeting contexts. The 'hi' replacement was done centrally but should be context-specific. The fix is to remove the word replacement from the domain or limit it to greeting contexts only.

kb_faqs.question: How long does a typical campaign setup and UAT take from kickoff?
kb_faqs.answer: A standard campaign setup includes internal kickoff (understanding use case), configuration, prompt testing, bulk lead upload, UAT execution, and final validation. Timeline varies from 5-9 days depending on client responsiveness and feedback incorporation. However, year-end and fiscal close periods can add delays. Key blockers are missing call scripts, delayed client approvals, and resource availability on client side.

kb_faqs.question: What feature request came from clients wanting to export leads from Convin Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: Some clients wanted the ability to export qualified leads directly from Convin Sense for external processing or human agent assignment. This request arose because the intended flow of human agents directly tapping leads in Convin Sense was not fully implemented or understood. Rather than building export functionality, the recommendation is to prioritize human agent onboarding and training within Convin Sense, which was the original design intent.

kb_faqs.question: What causes slow bulk lead uploads to get stuck at partial completion?
kb_faqs.answer: Bulk lead uploads can get stuck (e.g., stuck at 1k out of 15k leads) due to backend processing issues or API timeouts. When this occurs, the workaround is to retry the upload after confirming the file is valid. The system typically completes the upload on the second attempt. Note that NBA generation for some leads may still be in progress even after the upload shows completion, so allow 15-30 minutes for full processing.

Typical conversion rates from AI-only calling pilots range from 1-2% without human handoff. When human-in-the-loop is introduced (where warm/hot leads are transferred to agents), conversion uplift is expected but varies by vertical and lead quality. A key business case metric is comparing CPA (cost per acquisition) between AI-only and AI + human hybrid models. Clients see stronger ROI with hybrid approaches but require agent bandwidth.
kb_faqs.question: What conversion rates and ROI figures do clients typically achieve with Sense?
What should PMs do during kickoff calls to set a positive first impression?
kb_faqs.answer: PMs should start by summarizing their understanding of the use case in a few lines (learned from internal sales kickoff), confirm accuracy, then ask clarifying questions. This avoids asking clients to re-explain from scratch, which causes irritation if they've already covered it multiple times with sales. Proper internal KT between sales and PM is essential. This approach shows better ownership and keeps conversations crisp.

kb_faqs.question: Which LLM models perform best for lead qualification accuracy in Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: The standard progression is: GPT 4.0 → GPT 4.1 → Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite. Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite has shown improved accuracy for lead qualification when combined with updated prompts that include stricter guardrails. Migration from older models happens gradually across all domains. Always monitor quality closely after model updates and report any accuracy regressions immediately.

kb_faqs.question: What happens when human agents have low attempt rates on assigned leads?
kb_faqs.answer: When attempt rates fall below 50% (e.g., 22 of 44 leads attempted), it indicates either insufficient agent capacity, lack of motivation, or process gaps. This is a significant funnel leak. The solution is to have a direct conversation with the client stakeholder (e.g., agent manager) to understand blockers, reallocate agent workload, or adjust the lead distribution strategy. Continuing without fixing this results in poor campaign outcomes.

kb_faqs.question: Why is WhatsApp integration failing with duplicate pages and certificate generation errors?
kb_faqs.answer: Duplicate WhatsApp integration pages occur when the same business phone number is registered in multiple WABAs (WhatsApp Business Accounts). This prevents certificate generation on the Meta side. The fix is to ensure each phone number is used in only one WABA at a time. Remove the number from all other WABAs, then re-initiate integration. This is a configuration issue, not a product defect.

kb_faqs.question: What is the root cause of calls not happening from Exotel/telephony providers?
kb_faqs.answer: Calls may stop abruptly if postpaid Exotel accounts have unpaid bills. The solution is to ensure timely payment of Exotel invoices to maintain call service continuity. This is not a product issue but a vendor relationship/billing issue. Proactive monitoring of Exotel account status and bill schedules is recommended to prevent call drops during live campaigns.

kb_faqs.question: What is the workaround for tool edit UI not displaying for some campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Some campaigns show the 'Communication Limits' and tool edit sections while others do not, even though all campaigns were created using the same method. This appears to be a frontend issue from a recent release. The workaround is to contact product support to investigate the specific campaign configuration. A fix was released (ticket AC-1845), so ensure you're on the latest version.

kb_faqs.question: What causes the appointment tool to fail in capturing booking information?
kb_faqs.answer: Issues with appointment tool include: (1) tool definitions not properly linked to the prompt, (2) tool parameter naming mismatches between the prompt and the tool schema, (3) conversation context not being preserved across turns. The fix involves reviewing both the tool definition and the system prompt to ensure alignment. Comprehensive testing before campaign launch can catch these issues.

kb_faqs.question: What does 'call opening pace' refer to and why is it important?
kb_faqs.answer: Call opening pace refers to how quickly the bot delivers the opening line of the call. If the pace is 'off,' it means the bot is speaking too slowly or too quickly, creating an unnatural initial impression. This was observed in some pilots (ticket AC-1889) and requires TTS (text-to-speech) speed adjustment or prompt modification. The opening seconds set the tone for the entire conversation.

kb_faqs.question: Why is WhatsApp text not capturing context from the prior AI call?
kb_faqs.answer: When an AI call is completed, follow-up WhatsApp messages should reference key discussion points from the call. If WhatsApp text fails to capture this context, it indicates the conversation context from the call is not being properly passed to the WhatsApp NBA generation system. The fix involves ensuring the conversation summary from the call is available to the WhatsApp template system.

kb_faqs.question: What is the typical timeline for Genesys integration with Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: Genesys integration involves IP SEC tunneling setup followed by product integration work. Initial IP SEC setup is completed first, then product integration is prioritized in the roadmap. Based on pilot experience, expect 4-7 business days for initial product integration timeline once IP SEC is ready. A detailed timeline should be confirmed after scoping the specific Genesys configuration requirements.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What lead volume capacity should be planned for during onboarding?
kb_faqs.answer: Clients may initially quote lead volumes (e.g., '2k leads per day') but actual delivery is often lower (e.g., '80 leads per day matching specifications'). The recommendation is to run a pilot campaign with the actual available lead set first, monitor performance, then scale. Adjustments like removing test name restrictions may 2x or 3x available leads. Always validate actual vs. quoted volumes before committing to commercial terms.

kb_faqs.question: What causes latency and poor call quality in voice bot conversations?
kb_faqs.answer: High latency and poor call quality can stem from: (1) backend processing delays, (2) LLM response time issues, (3) network connectivity problems. This requires investigation from the infrastructure team to identify bottlenecks. Monitoring call recordings for quality issues and escalating them with specific lead examples helps the team identify patterns and prioritize fixes.

kb_faqs.question: What is the typical flow when clients have internal KT (knowledge transfer) requirements?
kb_faqs.answer: When a client's primary contact changes or has limited availability, internal KT must happen before the project resumes. This can add 1-3 days to the timeline. The recommendation is to directly involve the new stakeholder (e.g., new SPoC) in all subsequent communications and meetings. Ensure critical information is documented so transitions don't cause repeated delays.

kb_faqs.question: What should happen if agent calling is not happening on time despite lead availability?
kb_faqs.answer: If agents have assigned hot/warm leads but are not attempting calls, escalate to the client stakeholder immediately to understand blockers: insufficient agent availability, competing priorities, process misunderstanding, or tool issues. Provide daily scorecards to the client showing: leads assigned, attempts made, attempt rate. If attempt rate remains <50%, consider reallocating leads or extending timeline.

kb_faqs.question: How should product issues discovered during pilots be tracked and prioritized?
kb_faqs.answer: Product issues should be raised as Jira tickets with: (1) clear description, (2) affected client/domain, (3) specific lead links showing the issue, (4) screenshots or call recordings if applicable. Include relevant team tags (e.g., @Product, @Tech) to ensure visibility. Ticket status should be tracked daily, and blockers affecting live campaigns should be escalated to leadership for prioritization.

kb_faqs.question: What document or compliance requirement might delay a client's go-live?
kb_faqs.answer: Some clients require signed declarations or approval forms from admin teams before campaign launch. For example, a signed declaration on data usage or compliance may be needed. These approvals can be delayed if: (1) admin teams are on leave (especially during fiscal year-end), (2) forms are not clear, (3) multiple approvers are involved. Request all compliance documents early and follow up 2-3 days before planned go-live.

kb_faqs.question: How should campaign launch be timed relative to client resource availability?
kb_faqs.answer: Campaigns should not launch during periods when client resources are unavailable, such as fiscal year-end closing or major holidays. Even if systems are ready, client-side issues (approvals, agent availability, monitoring) will create friction. Coordinate launch dates with client stakeholders to ensure they have capacity for initial monitoring and issue escalation. A 2-3 day delay is preferable to a launch that suffers from poor handoff.

kb_faqs.question: What is the best practice for running campaigns on multiple phone numbers?
kb_faqs.answer: Campaigns should use multiple outbound numbers rather than a single number. Running on one number reduces pickup rates due to caller ID reputation build and rate limiting by carriers. Multiple numbers allow the system to distribute calls and improve connectivity. If a campaign is configured with only one number, discuss with product to add additional DIDs (direct inward dials) to the campaign.

kb_faqs.question: What is involved in preparing for a product KT session with customer-facing teams?
kb_faqs.answer: A comprehensive product KT session covers: (1) Appointment tool features, (2) Cross-campaign analytics and reporting, (3) NBA (Non-NBA) campaign flow, (4) Integration data encryption handling, (5) Bulk upload improvements, (6) Other recent releases. Sessions should be scheduled after major releases and recorded for teams unable to attend. Allow 1-1.5 hours and include hands-on walkthroughs of new features.

kb_faqs.question: What percentage of leads should typically be attempted by human agents after AI qualification?
kb_faqs.answer: Ideally, agents should attempt 80-100% of leads assigned to them, but real-world experience shows attempt rates of 50-70% are common. Attempt rates below 50% indicate significant funnel leakage. Target should be 70%+ attempts with proper follow-up and escalation to the client if this is not achieved. Low attempt rates combined with AI connectivity rates create a multiplier effect reducing overall campaign ROI.

kb_faqs.question: What causes repeated Meta/WABA payment method issues during campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: Meta occasionally shows OTP failures even with correct codes, forcing account managers to repeatedly contact card owners for retry. This is frustrating when the card owner is unavailable or busy. The solution is to switch to credit line (mCarbon) as the payment method, which doesn't require OTP. This is a permanent workaround recommended by the team and should be implemented during initial WABA setup.

kb_faqs.question: What is the difference between templated and non-templated WhatsApp messages?
kb_faqs.answer: Templated messages are pre-approved by Meta and sent to customers without an existing 24-hour conversation window. Non-templated messages are free-form and can be sent to numbers with active conversation history in the past 24 hours. If you send a non-templated message to a number that requires a template, delivery will fail. Always check: (1) has this number had prior chat?, (2) is template approved?, (3) is 24-hour window active?

kb_faqs.question: What is the recommended approach when clients want iterative prompt changes instead of UAT?
kb_faqs.answer: If a client requests repeated prompt changes (e.g., asking for adjustments after every demo), set expectations clearly: (1) incorporate feedback into a single test cycle, (2) run a UAT campaign with 100 sample leads to validate changes, (3) get sign-off before proceeding to full campaign. Avoid accepting changes after each feedback round, as this delays go-live indefinitely. The team should communicate this firmly but diplomatically.

kb_faqs.question: What training is needed for human agents when receiving AI-qualified leads?
kb_faqs.answer: Human agents need training on: (1) how to interpret AI lead classifications (hot/warm/cold), (2) context provided in the lead summary from the AI call, (3) how to access call recordings to understand customer intent, (4) follow-up talking points based on the call, (5) how to mark leads if they re-qualify or disqualify. Without this training, agents may not leverage the AI interaction data effectively.

kb_faqs.question: How should tool definitions be structured to ensure proper handoff to agents?
kb_faqs.answer: Tool definitions should clearly specify what information to capture (e.g., customer name, pincode, appointment time) and how to store it. The tool prompt must align with the system prompt to avoid confusion. If a tool is supposed to trigger only on explicit customer request, the system prompt must reinforce this. Test tools with sample conversations before campaign launch to validate they capture correct data.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: What validation steps should be done before uploading a lead CSV?
kb_faqs.answer: Pre-upload validation includes: (1) Check row count matches expected volume, (2) Verify all required columns are present and properly named, (3) Sample 10-20 rows for data quality (proper formatting, no missing fields), (4) Validate phone numbers (10 digits, no special characters), (5) Check for duplicate entries, (6) Ensure names are in title case not ALL CAPS, (7) Run a test upload with 10-20 leads if it's the first batch. This prevents failed uploads and poor bot performance.

kb_faqs.question: Why are Hinglish conversations showing language switching within a single message?
kb_faqs.answer: Language switching (e.g., English start then Hinglish middle, English end) occurs when: (1) the customer has prior chat history with mixed languages, which the system learns from, (2) free-form messages (non-templated) default to LLM language selection rather than configured language. The fix is to check if the number has prior chat history. If the customer has multiple prior messages in Hinglish, the system may persist that language choice.

kb_faqs.question: What should be included in the client artifact/training material for Sense?
kb_faqs.answer: Client-facing training materials should include: (1) product overview and capabilities, (2) supported use cases, (3) step-by-step campaign setup guide, (4) lead CSV format specifications, (5) key concepts (NBA, lead scoring, DND), (6) troubleshooting guide, (7) support contact information. This booklet should be customized for each client vertical and iterated based on feedback from early pilots.

kb_faqs.question: What is the expected connectivity rate for AI voice calls in different verticals?
kb_faqs.answer: Connectivity rates (successful call completion) typically vary by vertical and lead quality: financial services and education verticals see 40-60% connectivity, while collections and B2B see 20-40%. Rates depend on: (1) lead data quality, (2) phone number freshness, (3) time of day, (4) number of retry attempts configured. Always establish baseline connectivity benchmarks with the client during kickoff.

kb_faqs.question: What product improvements were identified during pilot deployments?
kb_faqs.answer: Key improvements identified: (1) Better CRM metadata handling in lead scoring to prevent false positives, (2) Language detection and switching for Hinglish conversations, (3) Tool edit UI consistency across campaigns, (4) WhatsApp health metrics and message limit defaults, (5) Bulk upload improvements for handling large lead sets, (6) Multiple number support for campaigns. These are being prioritized in the product roadmap based on pilot feedback.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: What is the difference between NBA and Non-NBA campaigns?
kb_faqs.answer: NBA campaigns automatically trigger follow-up actions (WhatsApp, calls) based on lead behavior or qualification. Non-NBA campaigns run a single outreach attempt without automatic follow-ups. Non-NBA campaigns require manual lead assignment or pre-configured retry windows. The choice depends on client preference: NBA is better for high-volume scalability, Non-NBA gives more control but requires manual management.

kb_faqs.question: What is the current backlog of integrations and features in the product roadmap?
kb_faqs.answer: As of the last update, key items in progress or planned include: (1) MCube Telephony Integration (in dev, ETA pending Mcube's actions), (2) Genesys custom integration (ETA ~3-7 days after IP SEC setup), (3) Custom lead archival workflows, (4) Call slot utilization tracking for campaign resume logic. Teams should check the product release notes regularly for updated ETAs.

kb_faqs.question: How should lead handoff SLA be communicated to clients?
kb_faqs.answer: SLAs for lead handoff should be explicit: (1) AI bot attempts call within X hours of upload, (2) if connected, lead is handed to human agent within Y hours, (3) human agent must attempt call within Z hours. Most clients have 2-4 hour expectations. Clearly communicate what happens if human agents don't attempt calls (lead recycles to bot, escalates, or expires). Track and report against these SLAs daily to maintain transparency.

kb_faqs.question: What causes repeated failures in generating appointment bookings from conversations?
kb_faqs.answer: Appointment booking failures occur when: (1) tool is not properly triggered due to prompt not instructing bot to use it, (2) customer's response doesn't explicitly confirm the appointment time/date, (3) tool schema doesn't match the prompt's expectation of required fields. The fix is to ensure: (1) system prompt includes clear instruction to use appointment tool when booking is discussed, (2) tool requires only essential fields, (3) test with sample conversations before campaign launch.

kb_faqs.question: What is the impact of calling the same lead multiple times on connectivity?
kb_faqs.answer: Multiple retry attempts on the same lead can degrade caller ID reputation and pickup rates after 2-3 attempts within 24 hours. Carriers may rate-limit or block numbers with excessive calling to the same number. Recommended retry logic: Day 1 (attempt 1), Day 1 evening (attempt 2), Day 3 (attempt 3), Day 7 (attempt 4). Avoid more than 1-2 attempts per day on the same lead.

kb_faqs.question: What is the process for onboarding a new project manager for a pilot?
kb_faqs.answer: New PM onboarding includes: (1) assigned to a specific account or vertical, (2) briefed on use case and client goals by sales team, (3) given access to relevant documentation and systems, (4) paired with experienced PM for initial campaigns, (5) enrolled in product training on Sense features, (6) assigned first kickoff with support from experienced team member. This typically takes 2-3 days before the PM is ready to lead independently.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: Lead Data & CSV Quality
kb_faqs.question: How should lead CSVs be formatted for optimal bot pronunciation and performance?
kb_faqs.answer: Optimal CSV formatting: (1) Names in Title Case (e.g., 'Rajesh Kumar', not 'RAJESH KUMAR' or 'rajesh kumar'), (2) Phone numbers as 10-digit integers (e.g., 9876543210, not '+91-9876-543210'), (3) Product/domain names in standard format (e.g., 'Bajaj Pulsar', not 'BAJAJ PULSAR'), (4) No special characters or extra spaces, (5) Standardized state/city abbreviations. This ensures TTS pronunciation is accurate and LLM processing is clean.

kb_faqs.question: What is a realistic ROI improvement from implementing AI voice bots vs. traditional calling?
kb_faqs.answer: Early pilot results show: (1) AI bots can qualify 40-60% of leads for human follow-up, saving agent time on unqualified leads, (2) when combined with human handoff, conversion uplift of 20-40% is typical (from 1-2% to 2-4%), (3) cost per acquisition (CPA) can improve 30-50% by screening out low-intent leads before human touch. Exact ROI depends on lead quality, conversion rates, and agent cost structure.

kb_faqs.question: What is the recommended cadence for client check-in calls during campaign execution?
kb_faqs.answer: During the first week of campaign execution, conduct daily check-ins (15-30 min) to monitor: (1) call connectivity, (2) lead quality issues, (3) human agent attempt rates, (4) any product issues. After week 1, shift to every-other-day check-ins if metrics are stable. Include stakeholders from client (agent manager, ops lead) and Convin (PM, support). Escalate blockers same-day to avoid funnel leakage.

kb_faqs.question: What connectivity and call success rates are typical across deployments?
kb_faqs.answer: Teams typically report 85-90% connectivity rates across pilot deployments. However, specific issues like SIP server disk space exhaustion (reaching 100% capacity) can cause complete call failures until resolved. Real-world performance varies by carrier, region, and infrastructure readiness—voice AI worker initialization failures have been observed when server resources are constrained.

kb_faqs.question: What happens when calling numbers show DND (Do Not Disturb) status?
kb_faqs.answer: When customer numbers are marked DND at the carrier level, Click2Call and outbound attempts fail consistently with 'Failed' status, regardless of retry attempts. This can occur on client-side (dialler configuration) or carrier-side (number purchased by different account). Root cause analysis requires checking with the carrier (e.g., Exotel) to verify number ownership and account status before resuming campaigns.

kb_faqs.question: What latency issues have been observed in bot calls and how are they diagnosed?
kb_faqs.answer: High latency (1.5+ hours delay) has been observed in WhatsApp message delivery when using certain SMS/WhatsApp gateways like Karix. This is a carrier-level issue, not a platform issue. Escalation to the carrier's tech team (e.g., tech-support@karix.com) is required. Read receipts typically resume within 24 hours post-resolution; campaigns should wait before resuming to ensure stability.

kb_faqs.question: What system-prompt changes improve NBA loop-breaking behavior?
kb_faqs.answer: System-level prompt modifications have been made to the app domain to improve loop-breaking behavior and reduce hallucinations. When such changes are deployed, teams should monitor for issues and report immediately if new anomalies appear. Changes can be reverted if they cause unintended side effects in specific domains.

kb_faqs.question: What are word replacement issues in TTS integration and how are they resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: Word replacement rules in domain configurations (e.g., 'par' replacement in Metropolis domain) can cause unintended text generation in bot opening lines. These are manageable by removing the offending rule from the word replacement list in the domain settings. This is self-service and does not require engineering involvement once the Jira fix is released.

kb_faqs.question: What causes Click2Call failures and how is it resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: Click2Call failures returning 'Failed' status consistently are typically caused by: (1) DND flags on the customer numbers at the carrier level, or (2) Numbers being purchased by a different account in the carrier system. Exotel API investigation is needed to confirm number ownership and account status. Workaround: Agents can call from personal numbers until resolved.

kb_faqs.question: Why do WhatsApp templates not appear in the human agent interface despite correct configuration?
kb_faqs.answer: WhatsApp template visibility on the agent side requires: (1) WhatsApp configured in campaign settings, (2) Templates selected in campaign config, and (3) All templates pre-approved in Meta/WhatsApp Business Account. If templates don't appear despite these steps, a ticket should be raised with product team to investigate the UI rendering logic and template sync between campaign config and agent interface.

kb_faqs.question: What causes bot voice to be inaudible to customers during calls?
kb_faqs.answer: Bot voice inaudibility has been traced to SIP server issues (e.g., disk space exhaustion preventing worker initialization) on the carrier side (Exotel). When this occurs, campaigns must be paused. Escalate to the carrier with API payloads for investigation. They will request detailed logs and request/response samples. Root cause may require server resource upgrades or automated cleanup scripts on the carrier infrastructure.

kb_faqs.question: What causes lead upload success counters to show 0 even when upload completes successfully?
kb_faqs.answer: Lead upload completion may show success = 0 while the popup confirms 'all leads uploaded.' This is a UI rendering bug where the success counter bar no longer increments progressively (0 → N). The upload itself is successful; this is a reporting/display issue. A ticket should be raised to fix the progress bar animation and final success count display.

kb_faqs.question: What causes queued calls to never attempt and show no further activity?
kb_faqs.answer: Queued calls with no retry activity despite retry mechanism set to 3 attempts may indicate a Non-NBA flow configuration issue or a queue processing bottleneck. This requires ticket investigation into the call queue handler and retry logic for that specific flow type. Check Jira ticket (AC-1947 example) with call logs to diagnose whether calls are stuck in queue or if retry logic is not triggering.

kb_faqs.question: What is the bug with variable deletion in agent training prompts?
kb_faqs.answer: When editing agent training sentences like 'A did {c}{d}', the system prevents deleting {c} without also deleting {d}. Variables are interdependent in the parsing logic. Workaround: Delete all dependent variables together or restructure the sentence. This is logged as a bug requiring engineering fix to allow granular variable removal.

kb_faqs.question: What causes NBA to trigger WhatsApp events when the system message says 'Start with AI Call'?
kb_faqs.answer: NBA is incorrectly triggering WhatsApp engagement even when the system message explicitly states 'No prior interaction with lead. Start engagement with AI Call.' This has been observed in ~10% of cases. Root cause is likely in the NBA decision logic not respecting the system message context. A ticket (AC-1958) should be raised to Lalit (NBA specialist) for investigation and fix.

kb_faqs.question: What causes CRM updates webhook to not trigger even when event conditions are met?
kb_faqs.answer: The CRM updates webhook is not firing in some cases despite webhook event conditions being met. This is a priority issue affecting lead data syncing to client CRMs. Investigation required to check: (1) Webhook condition matching logic, (2) Event payload generation, and (3) Delivery retry mechanism. Requires engineering ticket with detailed logs of missed events.

kb_faqs.question: What causes complete payload not being sent to client endpoints?
kb_faqs.answer: Webhook payloads are being truncated or sent incompletely to client API endpoints. This causes data loss in integration. Requires ticket (AC-1959 example) with sample payloads showing what is expected vs. what is received. Investigation should check payload serialization, size limits, and API integration layer.

kb_faqs.question: What causes WhatsApp messages to be generated incorrectly in content?
kb_faqs.answer: WhatsApp message content generation issues (ticket AC-1983, AC-1984) manifest as incorrect or incomplete message text being sent to leads. This can affect all leads if system-wide. Requires investigation into the LLM prompt, template selection, and variable substitution logic. May involve prompt tuning or dev fixes depending on root cause.

kb_faqs.question: What causes dynamic links sent via WhatsApp to be hallucinated/incorrect?
kb_faqs.answer: LLM hallucination causes incorrect meeting links to be generated and sent via WhatsApp when links are dynamic (not static). The bot generates plausible but wrong URLs instead of correctly inserting the actual link. This requires dev work to enforce link insertion at the template/variable level rather than relying on LLM generation. Estimated timeline: 1+ day for minor dev + release cycle.

kb_faqs.category: Client Learnings: NBA Loop & Retry Logic
kb_faqs.question: When should NBA be discarded and regenerated after a lead update?
kb_faqs.answer: When a lead update API is triggered mid-campaign, the existing NBA (Next Best Action) should be discarded and a new one generated with updated lead data. This is important for automated campaigns where lead preferences change. Implementation requires a feature flag/configuration and requires brainstorming on how to handle in-flight conversations. Ticket PS-1894 raised for this use case.

kb_faqs.question: What WhatsApp delivery issues occur with certain gateways and how are they resolved?
kb_faqs.answer: Karix WhatsApp gateway has shown 1.5+ hour delivery delays for NBA-initiated WhatsApp messages. This is a known carrier limitation with Karix. Workaround: Escalate to Karix tech-support@karix.com with complete logs and client's Exotel SPOC in CC. Resolution typically takes 24+ hours; read receipts resume after carrier-side fixes. Clients should wait 24 hours before resuming campaigns post-fix to ensure stability.

kb_faqs.question: What are the differences between utility and marketing WhatsApp templates?
kb_faqs.answer: Utility templates are for transactional/non-promotional messages (OTP, confirmations, alerts). Marketing templates are for promotional content. All templates must be pre-approved in the Meta WhatsApp Business Account before use. Approval times vary; delays in approval directly impact campaign launch timelines. Clients should submit templates early in UAT phase.

kb_faqs.question: What happens when WhatsApp Business Account profile details are incomplete?
kb_faqs.answer: Incomplete profile picture or account description in WhatsApp Business Account can prevent proper integration registration in Meta dashboard. This blocks campaign launch.
